message.content:
You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_60.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 60: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[60]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_60.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 60: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""Section Blueprint (v2 Loophole L5).
2	
3	Cross-section evidence assignment BEFORE parallel section writers begin.
4	Prevents the "everyone cites the same 5 sources" problem.
5	
6	The blueprint:
7	    1. Takes the outline (sections) + scored evidence pool
8	    2. Assigns primary/secondary evidence to each section via embedding similarity
9	       using search_keywords (Fix #2) not just title/description
10	    3. Enforces minimum evidence per section (or marks section as THIN)
11	    4. Core Source Bypass (Fix #3): high-authority evidence (standards, highly cited
12	       papers) bypasses the max-sections cap and is broadcast as global context
13	    5. Normal evidence capped at max_sections_per_evidence to reduce redundancy
14	    6. Provides the SourceRegistry mapping for consistent citation IDs
15	
16	Thread-safe: section writers read their assignment; no writes after build.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import logging
22	import os
23	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
24	from typing import Any, Optional
25	
26	import numpy as np
27	from dotenv import load_dotenv
28	
29	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.pooled_embedder import embed_with_pooling
30	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.source_registry import SourceRegistry
31	
32	load_dotenv()
33	
34	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
35	
36	
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	# Data classes
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	
41	# Fix R3-#5: Words-per-evidence-chunk for dynamic word budget
42	WORDS_PER_EVIDENCE_CHUNK = int(os.getenv("PG_WORDS_PER_EVIDENCE_CHUNK", "120"))
43	
44	
45	# I-deepfix-001 F5 (#1344): per-section WORD budget tracks the FULL routed basket payload.
46	#
47	# ``effective_target_words`` clamps the budget to ``min(outline_target, evidence_count * 120)``. The
48	# ``min(outline_target, ..)`` CEILING throttles a rich facet: once F1 routes 40 verified baskets to a
49	# section, the writer should develop one verified sentence per basket, but the ceiling caps the budget
50	# at the generic outline target (e.g. 800), truncating the payload. When this flag is ON the ceiling
51	# is dropped so the word budget tracks the full evidence payload (a 40-basket facet develops fully; a
52	# thin section still stays short via the evidence_count term). Read at CALL time (monkeypatch-testable).
53	# Default-OFF => the exact legacy ``min(target, evidence_budget)`` clamp (byte-identical).
54	def _word_budget_tracks_payload() -> bool:
55	    """True iff PG_WORD_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD removes the ``min(target_words, ..)`` CEILING from
56	    ``effective_target_words`` so the per-section word budget tracks the full routed basket payload
57	    (F5). Default-OFF => the legacy clamp (byte-identical)."""
58	    return os.getenv("PG_WORD_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD", "0").strip().lower() not in (
59	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
60	    )
61	
62	# Fix R3-#2: Placeholder for sections with zero evidence
63	EMPTY_SECTION_PLACEHOLDER = (
64	    "> [!NOTE]\n"
65	    "> No reliable evidence was retrieved for this section. "
66	    "This topic requires additional primary sources.\n"
67	)
68	
69	
70	@dataclass
71	class SectionSpec:
72	    """Specification for a single section with its evidence assignment."""
73	
74	    section_id: str
75	    title: str
76	    description: str = ""
77	    search_keywords: str = ""       # Fix #2: LLM-generated routing keywords
78	    target_words: int = 800         # outline target (may be overridden by effective_target_words)
79	    assigned_evidence_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
80	    secondary_evidence_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
81	    global_context_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # Fix #3: core sources
82	    is_thin: bool = False           # True if below min_evidence_per_section
83	    is_empty: bool = False          # Fix R3-#2: True if 0 evidence (skip LLM entirely)
84	    evidence_count: int = 0
85	    avg_relevance: float = 0.0
86	
87	    @property
88	    def effective_target_words(self) -> int:
89	        """Fix R3-#5: Dynamic word budget based on evidence payload.
90	
91	        Prevents the target-word contradiction: if the blueprint routes only
92	        2 chunks (~150 words of data) to a section with target_words=800,
93	        the LLM is forced to hallucinate 650 words of filler.
94	
95	        Formula: min(outline_target, evidence_count * WORDS_PER_EVIDENCE_CHUNK)
96	        Minimum floor of 150 words to avoid degenerate sections.
97	        """
98	        if self.evidence_count == 0:
99	            return 0
100	        evidence_budget = self.evidence_count * WORDS_PER_EVIDENCE_CHUNK
101	        # I-deepfix-001 F5 (#1344): when payload-tracking is ON the min(target_words, ..) CEILING is
102	        # dropped so a facet with many routed baskets develops fully (one verified sentence per basket).
103	        # Default-OFF => the exact legacy min(target, evidence_budget) clamp (byte-identical).
104	        if _word_budget_tracks_payload():
105	            return max(150, evidence_budget)
106	        return max(150, min(self.target_words, evidence_budget))
107	
108	    @property
109	    def should_skip_llm(self) -> bool:
110	        """Fix R3-#2: True if this section has no evidence and should not invoke LLM.
111	
112	        If True, the section writer should emit EMPTY_SECTION_PLACEHOLDER
113	        instead of calling the LLM, and the quality gate regex check should
114	        be skipped for this section.
115	        """
116	        return self.is_empty or self.evidence_count == 0
117	
118	
119	@dataclass
120	class BlueprintStats:
121	    """Statistics from the blueprint build process."""
122	
123	    total_sections: int = 0
124	    total_evidence: int = 0
125	    thin_sections: int = 0
126	    avg_evidence_per_section: float = 0.0
127	    max_evidence_per_section: int = 0
128	    min_evidence_per_section: int = 0
129	    unassigned_evidence: int = 0
130	    multi_assigned_evidence: int = 0  # assigned to >1 section
131	
132	
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	# Section Blueprint
135	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
136	
137	class SectionBlueprint:
138	    """Cross-section evidence assignment engine (L5).
139	
140	    Given an outline and a scored evidence pool, assigns evidence to sections
141	    using embedding similarity.
142	
143	    Fix #2 — Asymmetric Routing: If sections have `search_keywords` (generated
144	    by the outline LLM), we embed those keywords instead of the section
145	    title/description. This solves the vocabulary mismatch between abstract
146	    section titles ("Comparison of epoxy adhesion methods") and dense empirical
147	    evidence ("The 20mm dolly separated at 4.2 MPa").
148	
149	    Fix #3 — Core Source Bypass: Evidence from high-authority sources
150	    (score >= core_source_threshold) bypasses the max_sections_per_evidence
151	    cap. These are broadcast as `global_context_ids` to ALL sections that
152	    score above secondary_threshold. An ASTM standard or seminal paper
153	    must be available to Introduction, Methodology, Equipment, AND Conclusion.
154	    """
155	
156	    def __init__(
157	        self,
158	        min_evidence_per_section: int = 5,
159	        max_sections_per_evidence: int = 2,
160	        max_chunks_per_section: int = int(
161	            os.getenv("PG_BLUEPRINT_MAX_CHUNKS_PER_SECTION", "15")
162	        ),
163	        primary_threshold: float = 0.40,
164	        secondary_threshold: float = 0.25,
165	        core_source_threshold: float = float(
166	            os.getenv("PG_BLUEPRINT_CORE_SOURCE_THRESHOLD", "0.85")
167	        ),
168	    ) -> None:
169	        self._min_per_section = min_evidence_per_section
170	        self._max_per_evidence = max_sections_per_evidence
171	        self._max_per_section = max_chunks_per_section
172	        self._primary_threshold = primary_threshold
173	        self._secondary_threshold = secondary_threshold
174	        self._core_source_threshold = core_source_threshold
175	
176	        # Lazy-loaded
177	        self._embed_fn = None
178	
179	    def build(
180	        self,
181	        sections: list[dict[str, Any]],
182	        evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
183	        registry: SourceRegistry,
184	    ) -> tuple[list[SectionSpec], BlueprintStats]:
185	        """Build the section blueprint.
186	
187	        Args:
188	            sections: List of outline sections with title, description,
189	                      target_words, and optionally search_keywords (Fix #2).
190	            evidence: List of EvidencePiece-compatible dicts (from CRAG retriever).
191	            registry: Global source registry for citation ID mapping.
192	
193	        Returns:
194	            (specs, stats) — list of SectionSpec with evidence assigned, plus stats.
195	        """
196	        if not sections or not evidence:
197	            return [], BlueprintStats()
198	
199	        embed = self._get_embed_fn()
200	
201	        # Fix #2: Use search_keywords for routing if available,
202	        # fall back to title + description
203	        section_texts = []
204	        for s in sections:
205	            keywords = s.get("search_keywords", "").strip()
206	            if keywords:
207	                # Embed the LLM-generated routing keywords
208	                section_texts.append(keywords)
209	            else:
210	                section_texts.append(
211	                    f"{s.get('title', '')}. {s.get('description', '')}"
212	                )
213	
214	        # Build evidence texts (use statement or direct_quote)
215	        evidence_texts = [
216	            e.get("statement") or e.get("direct_quote", "")
217	            for e in evidence
218	        ]
219	
220	        # Fix R4-#1: Use pooled embedding for texts exceeding model's
221	        # 256-token max_seq_length. Evidence chunks are 1024+ tokens;
222	        # without pooling, the model truncates and all chunks from the
223	        # same document produce near-identical embeddings.
224	        all_texts = section_texts + evidence_texts
225	        all_embeddings = embed_with_pooling(all_texts, embed)
226	
227	        n_sections = len(sections)
228	        section_vecs = np.array(all_embeddings[:n_sections], dtype=np.float32)
229	        evidence_vecs = np.array(all_embeddings[n_sections:], dtype=np.float32)
230	
231	        # Normalize for cosine similarity
232	        section_norms = np.linalg.norm(section_vecs, axis=1, keepdims=True)
233	        section_norms = np.where(section_norms < 1e-8, 1.0, section_norms)
234	        section_vecs = section_vecs / section_norms
235	
236	        evidence_norms = np.linalg.norm(evidence_vecs, axis=1, keepdims=True)
237	        evidence_norms = np.where(evidence_norms < 1e-8, 1.0, evidence_norms)
238	        evidence_vecs = evidence_vecs / evidence_norms
239	
240	        # Similarity matrix: (n_sections, n_evidence)
241	        sim_matrix = section_vecs @ evidence_vecs.T
242	
243	        # Fix #3: Identify core sources that bypass the max-sections cap
244	        core_evidence_indices = set()
245	        for ev_idx, ev in enumerate(evidence):
246	            authority = ev.get("source_confidence", 0.0) or 0.0
247	            relevance = ev.get("relevance_score", 0.0) or 0.0
248	            # Core source: high authority OR exceptionally high relevance
249	            if authority >= self._core_source_threshold or relevance >= self._core_source_threshold:
250	                core_evidence_indices.add(ev_idx)
251	
252	        if core_evidence_indices:
253	            logger.info(
254	                "Blueprint: %d core sources bypass max-sections cap (threshold=%.2f)",
255	                len(core_evidence_indices), self._core_source_threshold,
256	            )
257	
258	        # Track how many sections each NON-CORE evidence is assigned to
259	        evidence_assignment_count = [0] * len(evidence)
260	
261	        # Build SectionSpec for each section
262	        specs: list[SectionSpec] = []
263	        for sec_idx, sec in enumerate(sections):
264	            scores = sim_matrix[sec_idx]
265	            ranked_indices = np.argsort(-scores)
266	
267	            primary_ids: list[str] = []
268	            secondary_ids: list[str] = []
269	            global_ctx_ids: list[str] = []
270	
271	            for ev_idx in ranked_indices:
272	                ev_idx = int(ev_idx)
273	                score = float(scores[ev_idx])
274	                ev_id = evidence[ev_idx].get("evidence_id", "")
275	                is_core = ev_idx in core_evidence_indices
276	
277	                # Fix #3: Core sources skip the assignment cap
278	                if is_core:
279	                    if score >= self._secondary_threshold:
280	                        global_ctx_ids.append(ev_id)
281	                    continue  # don't count against cap or add to primary/secondary
282	
283	                # Normal evidence: respect max_sections cap
284	                if evidence_assignment_count[ev_idx] >= self._max_per_evidence:
285	                    continue
286	
287	                if score >= self._primary_threshold:
288	                    primary_ids.append(ev_id)
289	                    evidence_assignment_count[ev_idx] += 1
290	                elif score >= self._secondary_threshold:
291	                    secondary_ids.append(ev_id)
292	                    evidence_assignment_count[ev_idx] += 1
293	
294	            # Fix R2-#2 (Context Bomb): Hard cap on total evidence per section.
295	            # Without this, 60 core chunks from an ASTM standard × 15 sections
296	            # = 900,000+ tokens sent to the API in one burst.
297	            # Strategy: keep all global context up to cap, then fill with primary,
298	            # then secondary. This prioritizes core sources over filler.
299	            raw_total = len(global_ctx_ids) + len(primary_ids) + len(secondary_ids)
300	            if raw_total > self._max_per_section:
301	                budget = self._max_per_section
302	                # Allocate: global first, then primary, then secondary
303	                g = global_ctx_ids[:budget]
304	                budget -= len(g)
305	                p = primary_ids[:budget] if budget > 0 else []
306	                budget -= len(p)
307	                s = secondary_ids[:budget] if budget > 0 else []
308	                global_ctx_ids, primary_ids, secondary_ids = g, p, s
309	                logger.debug(
310	                    "Section %s capped: %d -> %d chunks (g=%d, p=%d, s=%d)",
311	                    sec.get("section_id", sec_idx),
312	                    raw_total, len(g) + len(p) + len(s),
313	                    len(g), len(p), len(s),
314	                )
315	
316	            total = len(primary_ids) + len(secondary_ids) + len(global_ctx_ids)
317	            relevant_indices = [
318	                int(i) for i in ranked_indices[:max(total, 1)]
319	            ]
320	            avg_rel = float(np.mean([scores[i] for i in relevant_indices])) if total > 0 else 0.0
321	
322	            spec = SectionSpec(
323	                section_id=sec.get("section_id", f"sec_{sec_idx:02d}"),
324	                title=sec.get("title", f"Section {sec_idx + 1}"),
325	                description=sec.get("description", ""),
326	                search_keywords=sec.get("search_keywords", ""),
327	                target_words=sec.get("target_words", 800),
328	                assigned_evidence_ids=primary_ids,
329	                secondary_evidence_ids=secondary_ids,
330	                global_context_ids=global_ctx_ids,
331	                is_thin=total < self._min_per_section,
332	                is_empty=total == 0,
333	                evidence_count=total,
334	                avg_relevance=round(avg_rel, 4),
335	            )
336	            if spec.is_empty:
337	                logger.warning(
338	                    "Section '%s' has 0 evidence — will emit placeholder, skip LLM",
339	                    spec.title,
340	                )
341	            specs.append(spec)
342	
343	        # Stats
344	        counts = [s.evidence_count for s in specs]
345	        unassigned = sum(1 for c in evidence_assignment_count if c == 0)
346	        multi = sum(1 for c in evidence_assignment_count if c > 1)
347	
348	        stats = BlueprintStats(
349	            total_sections=len(specs),
350	            total_evidence=len(evidence),
351	            thin_sections=sum(1 for s in specs if s.is_thin),
352	            avg_evidence_per_section=round(float(np.mean(counts)), 1) if counts else 0.0,
353	            max_evidence_per_section=max(counts) if counts else 0,
354	            min_evidence_per_section=min(counts) if counts else 0,
355	            unassigned_evidence=unassigned,
356	            multi_assigned_evidence=multi,
357	        )
358	
359	        logger.info(
360	            "Blueprint: %d sections, %d evidence (%d core), %d thin, %.1f avg/section, %d unassigned",
361	            stats.total_sections, stats.total_evidence, len(core_evidence_indices),
362	            stats.thin_sections, stats.avg_evidence_per_section, stats.unassigned_evidence,
363	        )
364	
365	        return specs, stats
366	
367	    def get_evidence_for_section(
368	        self,
369	        spec: SectionSpec,
370	        evidence_pool: list[dict[str, Any]],
371	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
372	        """Retrieve the actual evidence dicts for a section spec.
373	
374	        Returns assigned evidence in order: global context first (core sources),
375	        then primary, then secondary.
376	
377	        IMPORTANT (Fix #4 — Attention Dilution):
378	            When building the synthesis prompt, group these by source_url/SRC-NNN
379	            and print source metadata ONCE per source, followed by all chunks.
380	            Do NOT repeat title+abstract per chunk.
381	        """
382	        by_id = {e["evidence_id"]: e for e in evidence_pool}
383	
384	        result: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
385	        # Global context (core sources) first — highest authority
386	        for eid in spec.global_context_ids:
387	            if eid in by_id:
388	                result.append(by_id[eid])
389	        for eid in spec.assigned_evidence_ids:
390	            if eid in by_id:
391	                result.append(by_id[eid])
392	        for eid in spec.secondary_evidence_ids:
393	            if eid in by_id:
394	                result.append(by_id[eid])
395	        return result
396	
397	    @staticmethod
398	    def group_evidence_by_source(
399	        evidence: list[dict[str, Any]],
400	    ) -> dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]]:
401	        """Group evidence chunks by source URL for prompt building.
402	
403	        Fix #4 — Attention Dilution: The synthesis prompt builder should
404	        call this to group chunks, then print source metadata ONCE followed
405	        by all chunks from that source. This avoids repeating the same
406	        300-word abstract 8 times when 8 chunks from one paper are routed
407	        to the same section.
408	        """
409	        groups: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {}
410	        for ev in evidence:
411	            url = ev.get("source_url", "unknown")
412	            if url not in groups:
413	                groups[url] = []
414	            groups[url].append(ev)
415	        return groups
416	
417	    # -- Internal ----------------------------------------------------------
418	
419	    def _get_embed_fn(self):
420	        """Lazy-load embedding function."""
421	        if self._embed_fn is None:
422	            from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
423	            self._embed_fn = embed_texts
424	        return self._embed_fn
425

1	"""Global Source Registry (v2 Loophole L1).
2	
3	Assigns monotonic SRC-NNN IDs to every unique URL ONCE, before
4	parallel Section Writers begin. Prevents citation collision when
5	multiple sections cite the same source concurrently.
6	
7	Thread-safe: uses threading.Lock for atomic ID assignment.
8	
9	Usage:
10	    registry = SourceRegistry()
11	    sid = registry.register(url="https://...", title="...", source_type="web")
12	    # sid == "SRC-001"
13	    entry = registry.get("SRC-001")
14	    bib = registry.to_bibliography()
15	"""
16	
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	import logging
20	import threading
21	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
22	from typing import Optional
23	
24	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
25	
26	
27	@dataclass(frozen=True)
28	class SourceEntry:
29	    """Immutable record for a registered source."""
30	
31	    source_id: str          # SRC-001, SRC-002, ...
32	    url: str
33	    title: str
34	    source_type: str        # web, academic, pdf, government, standard
35	    authors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
36	    year: Optional[int] = None
37	    venue: str = ""
38	    doi: str = ""
39	    domain: str = ""        # extracted from url for dedup
40	    authority_score: float = 0.0  # from source_confidence pipeline
41	
42	
43	class SourceRegistry:
44	    """Thread-safe global source registry.
45	
46	    Guarantees:
47	        - Each URL gets exactly one SRC-NNN ID (idempotent register)
48	        - IDs are monotonically increasing
49	        - Safe for concurrent access from parallel section writers
50	    """
51	
52	    def __init__(self) -> None:
53	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
54	        self._by_url: dict[str, SourceEntry] = {}
55	        self._by_id: dict[str, SourceEntry] = {}
56	        self._counter: int = 0
57	
58	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
59	    # Public API
60	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
61	
62	    def register(
63	        self,
64	        url: str,
65	        title: str = "",
66	        source_type: str = "web",
67	        authors: Optional[list[str]] = None,
68	        year: Optional[int] = None,
69	        venue: str = "",
70	        doi: str = "",
71	        authority_score: float = 0.0,
72	    ) -> str:
73	        """Register a source URL. Returns SRC-NNN (idempotent)."""
74	        normalized = self._normalize_url(url)
75	        with self._lock:
76	            if normalized in self._by_url:
77	                return self._by_url[normalized].source_id
78	            self._counter += 1
79	            sid = f"SRC-{self._counter:03d}"
80	            entry = SourceEntry(
81	                source_id=sid,
82	                url=url,
83	                title=title,
84	                source_type=source_type,
85	                authors=authors or [],
86	                year=year,
87	                venue=venue,
88	                doi=doi,
89	                domain=self._extract_domain(url),
90	                authority_score=authority_score,
91	            )
92	            self._by_url[normalized] = entry
93	            self._by_id[sid] = entry
94	            return sid
95	
96	    def get(self, source_id: str) -> Optional[SourceEntry]:
97	        """Lookup by SRC-NNN ID."""
98	        return self._by_id.get(source_id)
99	
100	    def get_by_url(self, url: str) -> Optional[SourceEntry]:
101	        """Lookup by URL."""
102	        return self._by_url.get(self._normalize_url(url))
103	
104	    def contains_url(self, url: str) -> bool:
105	        """Check if URL already registered."""
106	        return self._normalize_url(url) in self._by_url
107	
108	    @property
109	    def size(self) -> int:
110	        """Number of registered sources."""
111	        return self._counter
112	
113	    def all_entries(self) -> list[SourceEntry]:
114	        """All entries in registration order."""
115	        return [self._by_id[f"SRC-{i:03d}"] for i in range(1, self._counter + 1)]
116	
117	    def to_bibliography(self) -> list[dict]:
118	        """Export as bibliography list (for final report)."""
119	        bib = []
120	        for entry in self.all_entries():
121	            bib.append({
122	                "source_id": entry.source_id,
123	                "url": entry.url,
124	                "title": entry.title,
125	                "source_type": entry.source_type,
126	                "authors": entry.authors,
127	                "year": entry.year,
128	                "venue": entry.venue,
129	                "doi": entry.doi,
130	            })
131	        return bib
132	
133	    def to_citation_map(self) -> dict[str, int]:
134	        """Map SRC-NNN -> sequential citation number [1], [2], etc."""
135	        return {
136	            f"SRC-{i:03d}": i
137	            for i in range(1, self._counter + 1)
138	        }
139	
140	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
141	    # Internal
142	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
143	
144	    @staticmethod
145	    def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
146	        """Strip trailing slash, fragment, and lowercase for dedup."""
147	        url = url.split("#")[0].rstrip("/").lower()
148	        # Remove common tracking params
149	        if "?" in url:
150	            base, params = url.split("?", 1)
151	            clean_params = "&".join(
152	                p for p in params.split("&")
153	                if not p.startswith(("utm_", "ref=", "source="))
154	            )
155	            url = f"{base}?{clean_params}" if clean_params else base
156	        return url
157	
158	    @staticmethod
159	    def _extract_domain(url: str) -> str:
160	        """Extract domain from URL."""
161	        try:
162	            from urllib.parse import urlparse
163	            return urlparse(url).netloc.lower()
164	        except Exception:
165	            return ""
166

1	"""Semantic/NLI cross-document contradiction detector (I-ready-012 / #1079).
2	
3	The THIRD contradiction detector, complementing the numeric-regex detector
4	(``contradiction_detector``) and the NegEx/ConText rule-cue qualitative detector
5	(``qualitative_conflict_detector``). Those two cap detection recall at the rule
6	layer: a genuine prose-only directional contradiction with NO shared number and
7	NO lexicon cue — e.g. "adjuvant chemotherapy improved overall survival" vs
8	"...provided no overall survival benefit" — passes both silently. In a clinical
9	report that is the lethal-miss class (F12).
10	
11	This module adds an LLM-NLI pass that:
12	  1. clusters evidence rows by shared SALIENT content words computed from the raw
13	     row text (``cluster_candidate_rows``) — RECALL-oriented, independent of the
14	     rule extractors (which are blind to the no-number/no-cue rows). The cheap
15	     pre-filter bounds the O(n^2) judge cost; the judge provides precision.
16	  2. emits same-cluster row pairs (``extract_pairs``), hard-capped, highest-tier
17	     first.
18	  3. judges each pair (claim A vs claim B -> contradict/entail/neutral) and keeps
19	     ``contradict`` pairs above a confidence threshold (``detect_semantic_conflicts``).
20	
21	Design invariants:
22	  * ADDITIVE + fail-open: a detector / judge / import / budget error logs and
23	    skips; it NEVER aborts the sweep and NEVER weakens an existing gate. It can
24	    only ADD disclosures and (via PT08) make the release gate stricter.
25	  * Default OFF (``PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT``): flag-off is byte-identical — no judge
26	    is constructed and no network call is made.
27	  * The judge is INJECTED (a ``(claim_a, claim_b) -> (label, confidence)``
28	    callable), so the detector is fully offline-testable with a fake. The
29	    production judge (``get_default_judge``) reuses the family-segregated,
30	    cost-ledgered OpenRouter substrate (``PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL``, Gemma-4-31B by
31	    default) — the same two-family evaluator the strict_verify entailment judge
32	    uses — with a CONTRADICTION prompt. The strict_verify entailment path is NOT
33	    modified.
34	
35	Records are shaped (``SemanticConflictRecord``) so the existing ``contradictions.json``
36	merge consumes them, and they are routed by the caller into a dedicated report
37	disclosure block + the PT08 evaluator input (the numeric renderer is untouched).
38	"""
39	
40	from __future__ import annotations
41	
42	import concurrent.futures
43	import json
44	import logging
45	import math
46	import os
47	import re
48	import time
49	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
50	
51	# I-deepfix-001 (§9.1.8 anti-starvation): GLM ignores reasoning.effort, so a provider running reasoning
52	# long can eat the whole max_tokens budget and blank the verdict (a silently-missed contradiction). The
53	# shared leaf helper puts a NUMERIC reasoning cap (reasoning_cap << max_tokens, the proven D8-Mirror bound)
54	# on a glm judge body; a non-glm model keeps its byte-identical {effort} shape. Stdlib-light leaf import.
55	from src.polaris_graph.llm.judge_reasoning_block import build_judge_reasoning_block as _build_reasoning_block
56	# I-deepfix-001 (de-storm): SAME shared, env-driven bounded-concurrency cap as entailment_judge — the
57	# NLI conflict side-judge pins the SAME mirror chain, so it shares the ONE global slot pool that caps
58	# TOTAL glm-5.2 mirror POSTs in flight. Transport-only; the (label, confidence) logic is unchanged. LAW VI.
59	from src.polaris_graph.llm.judge_concurrency import acquire_judge_slot as _acquire_judge_slot
60	
61	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
62	
63	# --- configuration (LAW VI: all knobs are env-overridable) -------------------
64	_FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT"
65	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
66	
67	_ENV_MIN_OVERLAP = "PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT_MIN_OVERLAP"      # default 2 shared salient words
68	_ENV_MAX_PAIRS = "PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT_MAX_PAIRS"          # default 60 judged pairs
69	_ENV_MAX_ROWS = "PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT_MAX_ROWS"            # default 200 rows clustered
70	_ENV_MIN_CONFIDENCE = "PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT_MIN_CONFIDENCE"  # default 0.7
71	
72	_DEFAULT_MIN_OVERLAP = 2
73	_DEFAULT_MAX_PAIRS = 60
74	_DEFAULT_MAX_ROWS = 200
75	_DEFAULT_MIN_CONFIDENCE = 0.7
76	
77	# I-arch-002 (operator 2026-06-13): default to the sovereign open-weight evaluator GLM-5.1 (was the stale
78	# non-reasoning "google/gemma-4-31b-it" leftover — #1249/#1252; only env PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL had masked it).
79	_DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"  # I-beatboth-008 (#1285): mirror upgrade 5.1->5.2
80	_JUDGE_TIMEOUT_S = 30.0
81	# I-arch-006 HANG-J1 sibling (#1262): the semantic-conflict judge had the SAME bare-float per-read gap
82	# timeout that let a trickled / idle-open OpenRouter socket run UNBOUNDED. Tight read-stall (dead socket
83	# trips fast; keep-alives reset the timer so a slow-but-alive judge is unaffected) + bounded keepalive to
84	# reap half-open CLOSE_WAIT sockets. Transport-only — the fail-open ("neutral", 0.0) verdict logic that
85	# prevents a transient outage from FABRICATING a conflict is UNCHANGED. (LAW VI: env-driven.)
86	_JUDGE_CONNECT_S = float(os.getenv("PG_NLI_CONFLICT_CONNECT_S", "30"))
87	_JUDGE_READ_STALL_S = float(os.getenv("PG_NLI_CONFLICT_READ_STALL_S", "120"))
88	_JUDGE_WRITE_S = float(os.getenv("PG_NLI_CONFLICT_WRITE_S", "60"))
89	_JUDGE_POOL_S = float(os.getenv("PG_NLI_CONFLICT_POOL_S", "30"))
90	_JUDGE_MAX_KEEPALIVE = int(os.getenv("PG_NLI_CONFLICT_MAX_KEEPALIVE", "8"))
91	_JUDGE_KEEPALIVE_EXPIRY_S = float(os.getenv("PG_NLI_CONFLICT_KEEPALIVE_EXPIRY_S", "30"))
92	# I-arch-007 BUG-2 (verify-hang, 2026-06-17): the read-stall above (read=_JUDGE_READ_STALL_S) is a per-read
93	# GAP timeout that httpx RESETS on every received byte. OpenRouter/Cloudflare holds the NLI-conflict judge
94	# socket ESTABLISHED and TRICKLES keep-alive bytes (chunked keep-alives), so the gap timer never elapses and
95	# a SINGLE bare `self._client.post` in `_post_once` runs UNBOUNDED — observed freezing entire cQ72/cQ90
96	# verify-phase runs (the main thread parks forever in httpcore `read`, flat CPU / ep_poll). The sibling
97	# entailment judge (entailment_judge.py:115 HANG-J3), the credibility judge (credibility_judge_caller.py:104)
98	# and the 4-role transport (openrouter_role_transport.py:463) were ALL hardened with this exact HARD TOTAL
99	# per-call wall-deadline; this NLI-conflict side-judge was the one bare POST that slipped. Fix: bound the
100	# WHOLE call with `_post_with_total_deadline` (below); on timeout force-close the hung client + REBUILD a
101	# fresh one, then RAISE so the existing `call_side_judge_with_guard` retry/sentinel path runs — the timeout
102	# surfaces as the SAME conflict_unscored / fail-open outcome a transport failure already produces. Transport-
103	# only: the (label, confidence) verdict logic + the strict-fail-closed contract are UNCHANGED. LAW VI:
104	# env-driven; default 150s comfortably exceeds a real high-effort GLM-5.1 NLI call (~6-40s observed) while
105	# bounding the trickle hang.
106	_JUDGE_TOTAL_S = float(os.getenv("PG_NLI_CONFLICT_TOTAL_S", "150"))
107	
108	
109	def _post_with_total_deadline(client, endpoint, headers, json_body, total_s):
110	    """I-arch-007 BUG-2: run the blocking NLI-conflict POST under a HARD total wall-deadline.
111	
112	    httpx's ``read`` timeout is a per-byte GAP that a trickled keep-alive socket resets indefinitely;
113	    this bounds the WHOLE call so one hung judge POST can never freeze the verify phase. Runs the POST
114	    on a worker thread and waits at most ``total_s``. On timeout the client is force-closed (so the
115	    worker's hung read errors out and the thread exits) and ``concurrent.futures.TimeoutError`` is
116	    re-raised for the caller's force-close+rebuild+retry. Ports ``entailment_judge._post_with_total_
117	    deadline`` verbatim (the ``client.close()`` is guarded so a flaky close never masks the timeout).
118	    Returns the ``httpx.Response`` on success.
119	
120	    I-deepfix-001 (de-storm): the POST runs under the shared, env-driven bounded side-judge concurrency
121	    cap (``acquire_judge_slot``) so at most ``PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY`` provider POSTs (across all
122	    three side judges) are in flight at once — the burst can no longer 429-storm the mirror-chain lead.
123	    Transport-only; the (label, confidence) logic is unchanged.
124	    """
125	    with _acquire_judge_slot():
126	        ex = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)
127	        fut = ex.submit(client.post, endpoint, headers=headers, json=json_body)
128	        try:
129	            return fut.result(timeout=total_s)
130	        except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:
131	            try:
132	                client.close()  # force the hung socket closed -> the worker's blocked read unblocks + exits
133	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
134	                pass
135	            raise
136	        finally:
137	            # Deterministic executor teardown on EVERY exit path (success, timeout, or a non-timeout
138	            # client.post exception). wait=False so a still-unsticking worker never blocks us.
139	            ex.shutdown(wait=False)
140	
141	# I-arch-004 F19 (#1256, §9.1.8 "max_tokens ALWAYS go to the model REAL max — never starve; a generous cap is
142	# free, billed by usage not pre-allocated"): the NLI conflict judge is the SAME GLM-5.1 model pinned to the
143	# SAME LOCKED mirror provider chain (`get_role_provider("mirror")`, allow_fallbacks=False — see judge() below)
144	# as the main Mirror role, so its binding output cap is the SAME chain MIN the Mirror transport derived from a
145	# LIVE OpenRouter read 2026-06-14 (openrouter_role_transport.py:295, `_MIRROR_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN`): mirror
146	# chain order=[atlas-cloud, z-ai, baidu, novita, gmicloud] -> MIN max_completion_tokens 131072. A budget ABOVE
147	# 131072 would hard-400 on the z-ai/baidu/novita fallbacks under allow_fallbacks=False; so the default IS the
148	# chain MIN (the model REAL max for this pinned chain), env-overridable but CLAMPED DOWN to that ceiling. The
149	# old 2000 was the SMALL hardcode §9.1.8 prohibits — one provider hiccup from a finish=length truncation that
150	# returns the fail-open ('neutral', 0.0) and SILENTLY MISSES a real cross-document contradiction (the F19
151	# starvation class). Effort stays "high" (NOT xhigh): the Mirror GLM bake-off
152	# (openrouter_role_transport.py:700-705, mirror_glm_provider_bakeoff.py, 2026-06-14) proved xhigh is a NO-OP on
153	# GLM that lets reasoning eat the whole budget -> blank content -> the very collapse F19 closes. Kept as a
154	# LOCAL constant (NOT imported from roles) so this leaf retrieval module stays import-light. RE-DERIVE if the
155	# mirror chain is re-pinned in config/settings/openrouter_provider_routing.yaml to higher-cap-only providers.
156	_CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN = 131072
157	_DEFAULT_CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS = _CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN
158	
159	# Small, domain-aware stopword set. Salient-word overlap (NOT all words) keys the
160	# clustering, so generic connectives never group unrelated rows.
161	_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
162	    "the", "and", "for", "with", "was", "were", "has", "have", "had", "are", "but",
163	    "not", "from", "that", "this", "these", "those", "their", "its", "than", "then",
164	    "into", "onto", "over", "under", "between", "within", "without", "during", "after",
165	    "before", "while", "which", "when", "where", "what", "who", "whom", "how", "why",
166	    "study", "trial", "patients", "patient", "group", "groups", "results", "result",
167	    "showed", "shown", "found", "reported", "compared", "versus", "vs", "among",
168	    "using", "based", "data", "analysis", "associated", "may", "can", "also", "both",
169	    "more", "most", "less", "high", "low", "higher", "lower", "year", "years",
170	})
171	
172	# Tier ordering: highest-evidence pairs judged first (so the pair cap keeps the
173	# most decision-relevant conflicts). Unknown tiers sort last.
174	_TIER_RANK = {"T1": 0, "gold": 0, "T2": 1, "T3": 2, "T4": 3, "T5": 4, "T6": 5, "T7": 6}
175	
176	
177	@dataclass
178	class SemanticConflictRecord:
179	    """A cross-document semantic contradiction (type discriminator: ``semantic``).
180	
181	    Shaped for: (a) the merged ``contradictions.json`` dump; (b) the dedicated
182	    report disclosure block (subject + predicate + the two conflicting claims);
183	    (c) the PT08 evaluator gate (substring(subject) + substring(predicate) in
184	    report text). ``claims`` always has length 2 — the two conflicting sources.
185	    """
186	
187	    subject: str
188	    predicate: str
189	    claims: list = field(default_factory=list)  # [{evidence_id, text, tier, nli_label}, ...] (len 2)
190	    type: str = "semantic"
191	    severity: str = "review"
192	    nli_confidence: float = 0.0
193	
194	
195	# I-arch-005 B13 (#1257): the conflict side-judge's EMPTY-CONTENT collapse (a reasoning
196	# model returns a 200 with empty/None content) used to raise → the caller HELD the report.
197	# Operator-locked 2026-06-14 ("nothing shall hold the report"): on PERSISTENT empty (after
198	# the B14 retry) the unadjudicated pair is LABELED ``conflict_unscored`` and the run SHIPS.
199	# This is a DISCLOSED GAP, never an assertion: the judge could NOT adjudicate this pair — it
200	# does NOT say "no conflict" and it does NOT fabricate one. The faithfulness contract is
201	# unchanged; this only converts a HOLD into a disclosed label.
202	#
203	# The sentinel label the production judge returns when content stays empty after the B14
204	# retry. ``detect_semantic_conflicts`` recognizes it and emits a ``ConflictUnscoredRecord``
205	# (a disclosed-gap label) instead of skipping silently or raising.
206	CONFLICT_UNSCORED_LABEL = "unscored"
207	
208	
209	@dataclass
210	class ConflictUnscoredRecord:
211	    """A cross-document evidence PAIR the NLI conflict judge could NOT adjudicate.
212	
213	    Emitted (alongside the ``contradict`` records) when the side judge returns persistent
214	    empty content for the pair after the B14 retry. It is a DISCLOSED GAP, not a finding:
215	    it asserts nothing about whether the two claims conflict — only that the judge could not
216	    decide. The run-side glue (SWEEP lane) routes these into the disclosed-gaps artifact so
217	    the report ships with the gap surfaced rather than HELD. ``severity`` is fixed to the
218	    label so a consumer never mistakes it for a real ``review`` contradiction.
219	    """
220	
221	    subject: str
222	    evidence_ids: list = field(default_factory=list)  # the two pair members' evidence_ids
223	    reason: str = "conflict_judge_unavailable: empty judge content (could not adjudicate)"
224	    type: str = "conflict_unscored"
225	    severity: str = "unscored"
226	
227	
228	class ConflictJudgeUnavailableError(RuntimeError):
229	    """I-arch-004 F07 (#1249/#1252): the cross-document NLI conflict judge ERRORED while the
230	    strict-gate benchmark slate is active. Under strict gates a judge error must FAIL CLOSED
231	    — the run HOLDS for human review (the caller maps this to a run-level hold status) instead
232	    of the detector's additive fail-open silently dropping a POSSIBLE real contradiction as
233	    ('neutral', 0.0). This carries NO fabricated conflict: it signals "could not adjudicate",
234	    never "a conflict exists" — fabricating a phantom SemanticConflictRecord would itself be a
235	    faithfulness bug (and a false PT08 abort). Default (non-strict) path NEVER raises this."""
236	
237	
238	def semantic_conflict_enabled() -> bool:
239	    """True unless ``PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT`` is unset/falsey. Default OFF — flag-off
240	    is byte-identical (no judge constructed, no network)."""
241	    return os.environ.get(_FLAG, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
242	
243	
244	def _int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
245	    try:
246	        return int(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
247	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
248	        return default
249	
250	
251	def _float_env(name: str, default: float) -> float:
252	    try:
253	        return float(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
254	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
255	        return default
256	
257	
258	# I-wire-008 (#1322): provider-ROTATION for the NLI conflict side-judge. Unlike the entailment +
259	# credibility judges, this side-judge had NO rotation — it pinned ONE mirror host
260	# (allow_fallbacks:False) and the B14 guard re-POSTed the SAME host on every empty/no-choices
261	# attempt (the real "4x same-provider retry" the issue names: a flaky novita lead -> 3 same-host
262	# attempts -> conflict_unscored). Add the SAME rotation idiom the siblings use (advance through the
263	# mirror chain on a no-choices/empty 200), gated by the shared PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE flag.
264	# Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: same glm-5.2 model, next healthy host; the (label, confidence) logic + the
265	# fail-closed conflict_unscored contract are UNCHANGED.
266	_ENV_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE = "PG_JUDGE_PROVIDER_ROTATE"

279	    (``PG_JUDGE_UNHEALTHY_PROVIDERS``) drops flaky hosts. Returns ``[]`` when routing is

281	    try:
282	        from src.polaris_graph.roles.provider_routing import (
283	            filter_unhealthy,

295	def _no_choices_reason(data: object) -> str | None:
296	    """I-wire-008 (#1322): True-reason iff a 200 body cannot yield a verdict (an OpenRouter error
297	    envelope returned as a 200, or a missing/empty ``choices`` list). Used to (a) skip the phantom
298	    cost impute on a zero-completion transport fault and (b) trigger rotation. Mirrors
299	    ``entailment_judge._no_choices_reason``; duplicated to avoid the retrieval<->llm import cycle."""
300	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
301	        return "non_dict_body"
302	    if data.get("error"):
303	        return "error_body_200"
304	    choices = data.get("choices")
305	    if not choices or not isinstance(choices, list):
306	        return "no_choices"
307	    return None

310	def _row_text(row: dict) -> str:
311	    return str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or row.get("text") or "")
312	
313	
314	def _content_words(text: str) -> set:
315	    """Salient (>=3 char, non-stopword) lowercase tokens for overlap keying."""
316	    return {
317	        t for t in re.findall(r"[a-z0-9]+", (text or "").lower())
318	        if len(t) >= 3 and t not in _STOPWORDS
319	    }

321	
322	def _tier_rank(row: dict) -> int:
323	    return _TIER_RANK.get(str(row.get("tier") or ""), 99)
324	
325	
326	def cluster_candidate_rows(evidence_rows, *, min_overlap: int | None = None,
327	                           max_rows: int | None = None) -> list:
328	    """Group rows that share >= ``min_overlap`` salient content words (connected
329	    components). RECALL-oriented pre-filter, independent of the rule extractors.
330	
331	    Bounded: only the top ``max_rows`` rows (highest tier first) are clustered, so
332	    the O(n^2) comparison is capped at full scale. Returns a list of clusters
333	    (each a list of the original row dicts); singletons are dropped.
334	    """
335	    min_overlap = _DEFAULT_MIN_OVERLAP if min_overlap is None else min_overlap
336	    max_rows = _DEFAULT_MAX_ROWS if max_rows is None else max_rows
337	
338	    rows = [r for r in (evidence_rows or []) if _row_text(r).strip()]
339	    rows = sorted(rows, key=_tier_rank)[:max_rows]
340	    words = [_content_words(_row_text(r)) for r in rows]
341	
342	    n = len(rows)
343	    parent = list(range(n))
344	
345	    def find(x: int) -> int:
346	        while parent[x] != x:
347	            parent[x] = parent[parent[x]]
348	            x = parent[x]
349	        return x
350	
351	    def union(a: int, b: int) -> None:
352	        ra, rb = find(a), find(b)
353	        if ra != rb:
354	            parent[rb] = ra
355	
356	    for i in range(n):
357	        if not words[i]:
358	            continue
359	        for j in range(i + 1, n):
360	            if len(words[i] & words[j]) >= min_overlap:
361	                union(i, j)
362	
363	    groups: dict = {}
364	    for idx in range(n):
365	        groups.setdefault(find(idx), []).append(rows[idx])
366	    return [g for g in groups.values() if len(g) >= 2]
367	
368	
369	def extract_pairs(clusters, *, max_pairs: int | None = None) -> list:
370	    """Same-cluster row pairs, highest-tier first, hard-capped at ``max_pairs``."""
371	    max_pairs = _DEFAULT_MAX_PAIRS if max_pairs is None else max_pairs
372	    pairs: list = []
373	    for cluster in clusters:
374	        ordered = sorted(cluster, key=_tier_rank)
375	        for i in range(len(ordered)):
376	            for j in range(i + 1, len(ordered)):
377	                pairs.append((ordered[i], ordered[j]))
378	    # Rank pairs by best tier in the pair so the cap keeps the strongest evidence.
379	    pairs.sort(key=lambda p: (_tier_rank(p[0]) + _tier_rank(p[1])))
380	    return pairs[:max_pairs]
381	
382	
383	def _shared_subject(row_a: dict, row_b: dict) -> str:
384	    """Top shared salient words → a stable subject phrase (for PT08 + disclosure)."""
385	    shared = _content_words(_row_text(row_a)) & _content_words(_row_text(row_b))
386	    # Preserve appearance order in row A for readability.
387	    ordered = [w for w in re.findall(r"[a-z0-9]+", _row_text(row_a).lower()) if w in shared]
388	    seen: set = set()
389	    uniq = [w for w in ordered if not (w in seen or seen.add(w))]
390	    return " ".join(uniq[:4]) if uniq else "cross-document claim"
391	
392	
393	def detect_semantic_conflicts(
394	    pairs, judge, *, min_confidence: float | None = None,
395	    strict_fail_closed: bool = False,
396	    unscored_out: list | None = None,
397	) -> list:
398	    """Judge each pair; keep ``contradict`` pairs above ``min_confidence``.
399	
400	    ``judge`` is a ``(claim_a, claim_b) -> (label, confidence)`` callable, label in
401	    {"contradict","entail","neutral"}. Fail-open (default):
402	      * a per-pair judge error skips THAT pair (never fabricates a conflict);
403	      * a ``BudgetExceededError`` stops judging, KEEPS records found so far, and
404	        propagates as a clean stop signal (caught by the caller's fail-open block)
405	        — it never aborts mid-record.
406	
407	    I-arch-004 F07 (#1249/#1252) — ``strict_fail_closed`` (the strict-gate benchmark
408	    slate): a per-pair judge error must NOT silently skip (that could drop a real
409	    contradiction). Instead RAISE ``ConflictJudgeUnavailableError`` so the caller HOLDS
410	    the run for human review. This signals "could not adjudicate", NOT "a conflict
411	    exists" — no phantom record is ever fabricated. ``BudgetExceededError`` still
412	    propagates to keep-partial regardless of the flag (it is a clean stop, not an
413	    unadjudicated pair).
414	
415	    I-arch-005 B13 (#1257) — ``unscored_out`` (optional collector, operator-locked
416	    2026-06-14 "nothing shall hold the report"): the production judge returns the
417	    ``CONFLICT_UNSCORED_LABEL`` when its content stayed EMPTY after the B14 retry (the
418	    GLM reasoning-model collapse). When ``unscored_out`` is provided, that pair is appended
419	    as a ``ConflictUnscoredRecord`` (a DISCLOSED GAP — never a fabricated conflict, never a
420	    dropped one) and the loop CONTINUES. When ``unscored_out`` is None the unscored label is
421	    simply skipped (byte-identical to the pre-B13 "not contradict -> skip" path). This
422	    converts the empty-content HOLD into a label and is INDEPENDENT of ``strict_fail_closed``
423	    (an empty judge is always a label, never a hold).
424	    """
425	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import BudgetExceededError
426	
427	    min_confidence = _DEFAULT_MIN_CONFIDENCE if min_confidence is None else min_confidence
428	    records: list = []
429	    for row_a, row_b in pairs:
430	        text_a, text_b = _row_text(row_a), _row_text(row_b)
431	        try:
432	            label, confidence = judge(text_a, text_b)
433	        except BudgetExceededError:
434	            logger.warning(
435	                "[semantic-conflict] budget exceeded after %d record(s); "
436	                "stopping judge calls (fail-open, keep-partial).", len(records),
437	            )
438	            break
439	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — per-pair fail-open; never fabricate a conflict
440	            if strict_fail_closed:
441	                # I-arch-004 F07: under strict gates, an unadjudicable pair FAILS CLOSED ->
442	                # the caller holds the run for review (no silent drop, no fabricated conflict).
443	                raise ConflictJudgeUnavailableError(
444	                    f"conflict judge errored on an evidence pair under strict gates: {exc}"
445	                ) from exc
446	            logger.warning("[semantic-conflict] judge error on a pair (skipped): %s", exc)
447	            continue
448	        # I-arch-005 B13: the EMPTY-CONTENT collapse arrives here as CONFLICT_UNSCORED_LABEL
449	        # (the B14 guard already retried and returned a sentinel rather than raising). LABEL
450	        # the pair conflict_unscored (disclosed gap) and continue — never hold, never drop a
451	        # real conflict (none was adjudicated), never fabricate one.
452	        if str(label).strip().lower() == CONFLICT_UNSCORED_LABEL:
453	            if unscored_out is not None:
454	                unscored_out.append(ConflictUnscoredRecord(
455	                    subject=_shared_subject(row_a, row_b),
456	                    evidence_ids=[
457	                        str(row_a.get("evidence_id") or ""),
458	                        str(row_b.get("evidence_id") or ""),
459	                    ],
460	                ))
461	            continue
462	        if str(label).strip().lower() != "contradict":
463	            continue
464	        try:
465	            conf = float(confidence)
466	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
467	            conf = 0.0
468	        # Codex diff-gate P2: reject non-finite / out-of-range confidence. A NaN/inf from a
469	        # malformed judge response must NOT pass the threshold and fabricate a phantom conflict
470	        # — a phantom contradiction would falsely abort a legitimate run via PT08. (NaN < x is
471	        # False, so a bare `conf < min_confidence` would let NaN slip through; guard explicitly.)
472	        if not math.isfinite(conf) or not (0.0 <= conf <= 1.0):
473	            logger.warning("[semantic-conflict] dropping pair with non-finite/out-of-range "
474	                           "confidence %r (fail-safe, never fabricate a conflict)", confidence)
475	            continue
476	        if conf < min_confidence:
477	            continue
478	        subject = _shared_subject(row_a, row_b)
479	        predicate = "cross-document directional disagreement"
480	        # Each claim carries evidence_id + predicate + a finite value (0.0) so a
481	        # contradictions.json holding a semantic record stays loadable by
482	        # audit_ir.loader._parse_contradiction_claim (which REQUIRES those three keys
483	        # and does float(value)). Semantic conflicts are prose-only — there is no
484	        # numeric value — so value is the finite sentinel 0.0; the `type:"semantic"`
485	        # discriminator + `nli_label` distinguish them from numeric records downstream.
486	        records.append(SemanticConflictRecord(
487	            subject=subject,
488	            predicate=predicate,
489	            claims=[
490	                {"evidence_id": str(row_a.get("evidence_id") or ""), "predicate": predicate,
491	                 "value": 0.0, "text": text_a, "tier": str(row_a.get("tier") or ""),
492	                 "nli_label": "contradict"},
493	                {"evidence_id": str(row_b.get("evidence_id") or ""), "predicate": predicate,
494	                 "value": 0.0, "text": text_b, "tier": str(row_b.get("tier") or ""),
495	                 "nli_label": "contradict"},
496	            ],
497	            nli_confidence=conf,
498	        ))
499	    return records
500	
501	
502	def detect_semantic_conflicts_for_rows(
503	    evidence_rows, judge=None, *, strict_fail_closed: bool = False,
504	) -> list:
505	    """End-to-end convenience: cluster -> pairs -> judge. Used by the sweep block.
506	
507	    If ``judge`` is None the production default judge is lazily constructed
508	    (``get_default_judge``). Returns ``list[SemanticConflictRecord]``.
509	
510	    I-arch-004 F07 (#1249/#1252): ``strict_fail_closed`` (the strict-gate benchmark
511	    slate) makes the default production judge RAISE on a transport/parse error rather
512	    than return its additive fail-open ('neutral', 0.0), and makes the pair loop RAISE
513	    ``ConflictJudgeUnavailableError`` so the caller HOLDS the run (never a silent drop,
514	    never a fabricated conflict). Default False => byte-identical additive fail-open.
515	    """
516	    clusters = cluster_candidate_rows(
517	        evidence_rows,
518	        min_overlap=_int_env(_ENV_MIN_OVERLAP, _DEFAULT_MIN_OVERLAP),
519	        max_rows=_int_env(_ENV_MAX_ROWS, _DEFAULT_MAX_ROWS),
520	    )
521	    if not clusters:
522	        return []
523	    pairs = extract_pairs(clusters, max_pairs=_int_env(_ENV_MAX_PAIRS, _DEFAULT_MAX_PAIRS))
524	    if not pairs:
525	        return []
526	    if judge is None:
527	        judge = get_default_judge(strict_fail_closed=strict_fail_closed)
528	    return detect_semantic_conflicts(
529	        pairs, judge,
530	        min_confidence=_float_env(_ENV_MIN_CONFIDENCE, _DEFAULT_MIN_CONFIDENCE),
531	        strict_fail_closed=strict_fail_closed,
532	    )
533	
534	
535	def detect_semantic_conflicts_for_rows_with_unscored(
536	    evidence_rows, judge=None, *, strict_fail_closed: bool = False,
537	) -> tuple:
538	    """I-arch-005 B13 (#1257): cluster -> pairs -> judge, returning BOTH the contradict
539	    records AND the ``conflict_unscored`` disclosed-gap labels.
540	
541	    Returns ``(list[SemanticConflictRecord], list[ConflictUnscoredRecord])``. The unscored
542	    list carries the pairs whose judge content stayed EMPTY after the B14 retry — a DISCLOSED
543	    GAP the run-side glue (SWEEP lane) routes into the disclosed-gaps artifact so the report
544	    SHIPS rather than HELD. Identical clustering/pairing/threshold logic as
545	    ``detect_semantic_conflicts_for_rows``; the ONLY difference is it threads the
546	    ``unscored_out`` collector through so the empty-content label is surfaced rather than
547	    silently skipped. The legacy ``detect_semantic_conflicts_for_rows`` stays byte-identical
548	    (no collector) so its existing caller is untouched until SWEEP opts into this entry point.
549	    """
550	    clusters = cluster_candidate_rows(
551	        evidence_rows,
552	        min_overlap=_int_env(_ENV_MIN_OVERLAP, _DEFAULT_MIN_OVERLAP),
553	        max_rows=_int_env(_ENV_MAX_ROWS, _DEFAULT_MAX_ROWS),
554	    )
555	    if not clusters:
556	        return [], []
557	    pairs = extract_pairs(clusters, max_pairs=_int_env(_ENV_MAX_PAIRS, _DEFAULT_MAX_PAIRS))
558	    if not pairs:
559	        return [], []
560	    if judge is None:
561	        judge = get_default_judge(strict_fail_closed=strict_fail_closed)
562	    unscored: list = []
563	    records = detect_semantic_conflicts(
564	        pairs, judge,
565	        min_confidence=_float_env(_ENV_MIN_CONFIDENCE, _DEFAULT_MIN_CONFIDENCE),
566	        strict_fail_closed=strict_fail_closed,
567	        unscored_out=unscored,
568	    )
569	    return records, unscored
