572	# --- production judge (isolated; reuses the openrouter cost/family substrate) ---
573	
574	_CONTRADICTION_PROMPT = """You are a strict cross-document contradiction judge. You are given two independent CLAIMS, each from a different source document, about a related subject. Decide their logical relation.
575	
576	Rules:
577	- CONTRADICT: the two claims cannot both be true for the same population/endpoint — one asserts something the other explicitly denies or reverses (e.g. "improved overall survival" vs "no overall survival benefit"; "first-line therapy" vs "reserved for refractory cases").
578	- ENTAIL: the claims agree, or one restates / refines the other.
579	- NEUTRAL: the claims are about different things, or could both be true (different populations, endpoints, doses, or time points), or there is not enough overlap to judge a conflict.
580	
581	Be conservative: only answer CONTRADICT when the disagreement is direct and on the same subject. Return STRICT JSON only, no prose:
582	{{"verdict": "CONTRADICT" | "ENTAIL" | "NEUTRAL", "confidence": <number 0.0-1.0>}}
583	
584	CLAIM A:
585	{claim_a}
586	
587	CLAIM B:
588	{claim_b}
589	
590	JSON:"""
591	
592	
593	def _extract_first_json_object(content: object) -> dict:
594	    """I-arch-011 (B12): parse the FIRST complete JSON object out of a possibly-garbled
595	    NLI-conflict judge response and return it as a dict.
596	
597	    Motivating fault: a "garbled-200" = a valid verdict object followed by trailing
598	    reasoning text, e.g. ``{"verdict": "NEUTRAL", "confidence": 0.1}\\nReasoning: ...``.
599	    A bare ``json.loads(content)`` raises ``json.JSONDecodeError: Extra data: line 2 ...``
600	    on that input, which the ``except`` block treats as a transport/parse failure (a HOLD
601	    under strict gates, or a fail-open neutral otherwise) — throwing away a salvageable
602	    verdict.
603	
604	    Implementation: walk every ``{`` index and use ``json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode`` —
605	    the stdlib decoder that parses ONE complete value and ignores trailing data. This is
606	    brace-/quote-/escape-correct (a naive depth counter miscounts braces inside string
607	    values). The first ``{`` that decodes to a dict wins, so a response with prose BEFORE
608	    the JSON object is also rescued.
609	
610	    Selection rule: with ``response_format`` removed (B14), a prose-wrapped or multi-object
611	    body is now the normal case, so "first dict wins" could select the WRONG object (e.g. a
612	    leading ``{"note": "..."}`` ahead of the real ``{"verdict": ...}``). On THIS strict
613	    consumer that is faithfulness-relevant: a non-verdict first dict would yield verdict=""
614	    -> ``neutral`` -> ``("neutral", 0.0)``, silently DROPPING a real contradiction that
615	    pre-fix failed closed (strict HOLD). To prevent that fail-closed -> fail-open regression,
616	    this returns the first complete object that actually CARRIES the required ``"verdict"``
617	    field, and RAISES if the scan finds none. The scan advances past each decoded object by
618	    its end offset (not one char) so a nested sub-object is never mis-selected.
619	
620	    Fail-CLOSED contract (faithfulness): this function RAISES (never returns a default /
621	    neutral / partial) on empty/None/non-str content, on content with no ``{``, on a lone
622	    ``{`` or otherwise malformed/partial JSON, on a first value that is not a JSON object,
623	    AND on a valid object that lacks ``"verdict"``. The caller's existing fail-closed
624	    behaviour (strict -> HOLD, non-strict -> fail-open neutral) is therefore preserved
625	    byte-for-byte for every genuine failure — only the valid-verdict-object-plus-trailing-
626	    text case is newly rescued.
627	
628	    NOTE: this is intentionally duplicated from ``llm.entailment_judge`` rather than
629	    cross-imported, to avoid a retrieval<->llm import cycle (lane file-scope + LAW V).
630	    """
631	    if not isinstance(content, str) or not content.strip():
632	        raise ValueError("empty or non-str judge content")
633	    decoder = json.JSONDecoder()
634	    search_from = 0
635	    while True:
636	        # Scan for the next TOP-LEVEL JSON value, taking the EARLIEST of the next "{" or "[" so
637	        # we raw_decode the OUTERMOST value and NEVER descend into a container's interior.
638	        obj_idx = content.find("{", search_from)
639	        arr_idx = content.find("[", search_from)
640	        candidates = [i for i in (obj_idx, arr_idx) if i != -1]
641	        if not candidates:
642	            raise ValueError("no verdict-bearing JSON object found in judge content")
643	        start = min(candidates)
644	        try:
645	            value, end = decoder.raw_decode(content, start)
646	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
647	            # FAIL-CLOSED (Codex P1): a "{"/"[" that does NOT begin a COMPLETE JSON value means
648	            # malformed / partial / truncated content. Do NOT advance into its interior — that
649	            # would salvage a nested verdict out of a malformed envelope (e.g.
650	            # '{"wrapper":{"verdict":"NEUTRAL"}') and turn the strict fail-closed path into a
651	            # fail-open one. Raise so the caller's existing strict-HOLD / neutral path fires.
652	            raise ValueError(
653	                f"malformed JSON in judge content at offset {start}: {exc}"
654	            ) from exc
655	        if isinstance(value, dict) and "verdict" in value:
656	            return value
657	        # A COMPLETE value that is NOT a verdict object — a non-verdict object, an ARRAY (a
658	        # verdict nested in [ ... ] must FAIL CLOSED, not be pulled out — Codex iter-3 P1), or a
659	        # scalar — is skipped PAST by its end offset; we keep scanning at the TOP LEVEL and never
660	        # descend inside it. A verdict buried in a container is treated as absent (fail-closed).
661	        search_from = end
662	
663	
664	class _SemanticContradictionJudge:
665	    """Synchronous httpx wrapper around a cross-document contradiction call.
666	
667	    Mirrors ``llm.entailment_judge._EntailmentJudge`` (same two-family evaluator
668	    model, the same ``openrouter_client`` cost/budget helpers, family segregation
669	    enforced at construction) but with a CONTRADICTION prompt and a (label,
670	    confidence) return. Kept SEPARATE from ``_EntailmentJudge`` so the
671	    faithfulness-critical strict_verify entailment path is never touched.
672	    """
673	
674	    def __init__(self, *, strict_fail_closed: bool = False) -> None:
675	        import httpx
676	
677	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import check_family_segregation
678	
679	        # I-arch-004 F07 (#1249/#1252): under the strict-gate benchmark slate, a
680	        # transport/parse error must RAISE (caller holds the run) instead of returning the
681	        # additive fail-open ('neutral', 0.0) that silently drops a possible real conflict.
682	        self._strict_fail_closed = bool(strict_fail_closed)
683	        api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
684	        if not api_key:
685	            raise RuntimeError("PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
686	        self._api_key = api_key
687	        base_url = os.environ.get(
688	            "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
689	        ).rstrip("/")
690	        self._endpoint = f"{base_url}/chat/completions"
691	        self._model = os.environ.get("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL", _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL)
692	        # Two-family invariant (§9.1.1): the conflict judge is an evaluator-family
693	        # call and MUST differ from the generator family — raises at construction.
694	        check_family_segregation(evaluator_model=self._model)
695	        # I-arch-006 HANG-J1 sibling (#1262): explicit tight read-stall + bounded keepalive (see the
696	        # constant block above) replaces the bare-float per-read 30s gap that let a trickled judge POST
697	        # run unbounded. Verdict logic unchanged.
698	        # I-arch-007 BUG-2 (verify-hang): the HANG-J3-style TOTAL wall-deadline (see judge() ->
699	        # _post_with_total_deadline) bounds a TRICKLED socket the per-read gap alone cannot; build via
700	        # _build_client so the timeout path can force-close + REBUILD a fresh client after a hang.
701	        self._client = self._build_client()
702	
703	    def _build_client(self):
704	        """Construct the NLI-conflict httpx client.
705	
706	        BUG 3 (X509 SSL race): share the process-wide cert-verifying SSLContext so concurrent verify-path
707	        client builds never re-parse the PEM bundle (the ``[X509] PEM lib`` race). TLS verification stays
708	        ENABLED. I-arch-007 BUG-2: the HANG-J3-style total-deadline path (judge()) force-closes + rebuilds
709	        through here after a trickle hang. Verdict logic untouched."""
710	        import httpx  # local import: avoid forcing the dep when off
711	
712	        from src.utils.shared_ssl_context import get_shared_ssl_context
713	        return httpx.Client(
714	            verify=get_shared_ssl_context(),
715	            timeout=httpx.Timeout(
716	                connect=_JUDGE_CONNECT_S,
717	                read=_JUDGE_READ_STALL_S,
718	                write=_JUDGE_WRITE_S,
719	                pool=_JUDGE_POOL_S,
720	            ),
721	            limits=httpx.Limits(
722	                max_keepalive_connections=_JUDGE_MAX_KEEPALIVE,
723	                keepalive_expiry=_JUDGE_KEEPALIVE_EXPIRY_S,
724	            ),
725	        )
726	
727	    def judge(self, claim_a: str, claim_b: str) -> tuple:
728	        """Return ``(label, confidence)`` with label in {contradict,entail,neutral}.
729	
730	        Fail-open on API/parse error → ("neutral", 0.0) so a transient outage never
731	        FABRICATES a conflict. ``BudgetExceededError`` is re-raised (the caller
732	        stops + keeps partial) — never masked as a neutral result.
733	        """
734	        from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc
735	
736	        prompt = _CONTRADICTION_PROMPT.format(claim_a=claim_a, claim_b=claim_b)
737	        # I-arch-002 (#1251 sibling): GLM-5.1 is a REASONING model; at max_tokens=60 it truncated mid-
738	        # reasoning -> EMPTY content -> json.loads(None) NoneType -> fail-open neutral (silently misses real
739	        # conflicts). Operator 2026-06-13: reasoning stays MAX. Un-starve so high-effort reasoning completes
740	        # AND emits the JSON verdict; any sub-max/off effort is coerced UP to high. Env-overridable (LAW VI).
741	        _sc_effort = (os.environ.get("PG_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_REASONING_EFFORT", "").strip().lower()
742	                      or "high")
743	        if _sc_effort not in ("high", "xhigh"):
744	            _sc_effort = "high"
745	        # I-arch-004 F19 (§9.1.8): default to the GLM-5.1 mirror-chain MIN (model REAL max for the pinned
746	        # chain), env-overridable but CLAMPED DOWN to that ceiling so a bad override can never hard-400 the
747	        # judge under allow_fallbacks=False (the old hardcoded 2000 was the starvation-class small cap).
748	        try:
749	            _sc_maxtok = max(256, int(os.environ.get("PG_SEMANTIC_CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS", _DEFAULT_CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS)
750	                                      or _DEFAULT_CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS))
751	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
752	            _sc_maxtok = _DEFAULT_CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS
753	        _sc_maxtok = min(_sc_maxtok, _CONFLICT_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN)
754	        json_body: dict = {
755	            "model": self._model,
756	            "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
757	            "temperature": 0.0,
758	            "max_tokens": _sc_maxtok,
759	            # I-deepfix-001 (§9.1.8): a glm judge gets a NUMERIC reasoning cap (reasoning_cap << _sc_maxtok)
760	            # so a provider that runs reasoning long cannot blank the verdict; a non-glm model keeps the
761	            # effort shape. Operator 2026-06-13: reasoning stays ON either way (never disabled).
762	            "reasoning": _build_reasoning_block(self._model, _sc_effort, _sc_maxtok),
763	            # I-arch-011 (B14): no `response_format: {type: json_object}`. That structured-output
764	            # flag makes the novita and gmicloud mirror hosts return 404, collapsing the 4-host
765	            # mirror chain to 2. The brace-aware _extract_first_json_object parser (below) recovers
766	            # the JSON verdict from the now-unconstrained response, so all 4 providers route.
767	        }
768	        # I-arch-004 F09: route via "mirror" (the LOCKED 4-role key), NOT the RETIRED "evaluator"
769	        # key. The preflight-resolved role_provider_map only carries generator/mirror/sentinel/judge
770	        # (pathB_runner._LOCKED_ROLES); the legacy "evaluator" key is absent, so get_role_provider(
771	        # "evaluator") returned None -> NO provider pin -> this NLI conflict judge FREE-ROUTED to an
772	        # unpinned provider instead of the locked mirror chain. Per polaris_runtime_lock.yaml:
773	        # legacy_compat the retired evaluator role maps_to_role: mirror (GLM-5.1), so the side-judge
774	        # pins to the SAME provider chain as the main mirror role (allow_fallbacks=False,
775	        # require_parameters=True).
776	        try:
777	            from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb_for_routing
778	            _gate_provider = _pathb_for_routing.get_role_provider("mirror")
779	        except Exception:
780	            _gate_provider = None
781	        # I-arch-002 (#1250): operator-directed — skip the single-provider pin when
782	        # PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS is set so the open-weight evaluator model free-routes to its
783	        # fastest provider (the model is the sovereign unit; hosting provider may be US/China).
784	        _free_route = os.environ.get("PG_ROLE_ALLOW_FALLBACKS", "").strip().lower() in (
785	            "1", "true", "yes", "on",
786	        )
787	        # I-wire-008 (#1322): provider-rotation chain (the side-judge had none). When enabled,
788	        # derive the health-filtered mirror `order` straight from the YAML (independent of the
789	        # pathB contextvar, like the siblings) and pin the LEAD; the B14 guard's per-attempt
790	        # re-POST then advances through the chain via `_rotate_provider` below. The YAML LEAD ==
791	        # the prior single-host pin lead, so attempt 1 is identical to the pre-fix pin.
792	        _rotate_on = _judge_provider_rotation_enabled() and not _free_route
793	        _rotate_chain = _mirror_provider_chain() if _rotate_on else []
794	        if len(_rotate_chain) <= 1:
795	            _rotate_chain = []  # nothing to rotate through -> single-host pin below
796	        _provider_cursor = {"i": 0, "calls": 0}
797	        if _rotate_chain:
798	            json_body["provider"] = {
799	                "order": [_rotate_chain[0]], "allow_fallbacks": False, "require_parameters": True,
800	            }
801	        elif _gate_provider and not _free_route:
802	            json_body["provider"] = {
803	                "order": [_gate_provider],
804	                "allow_fallbacks": False,
805	                "require_parameters": True,
806	            }
807	
808	        def _rotate_provider() -> None:
809	            """Advance the pinned provider to the next health-filtered mirror host. Called at the
810	            top of each B14 guard re-attempt (calls>0) so a flaky/no-choices lead is SKIPPED rather
811	            than re-POSTed. No-op when rotation is off or the chain is exhausted. SINGLE-WRITER safe:
812	            this judge runs in the SERIAL P5 claim-graph step (see the TimeoutError note in
813	            `_post_once`), so the shared `json_body` mutation never races a sibling worker."""
814	            if not _rotate_chain or "provider" not in json_body:
815	                return
816	            if _provider_cursor["i"] >= len(_rotate_chain) - 1:
817	                return  # exhausted the chain — let the bounded guard fail closed (conflict_unscored)
818	            _provider_cursor["i"] += 1
819	            json_body["provider"]["order"] = [_rotate_chain[_provider_cursor["i"]]]
820	            logger.warning(
821	                "[semantic-conflict] provider-rotate -> %s (chain pos %d/%d)",
822	                _rotate_chain[_provider_cursor["i"]], _provider_cursor["i"] + 1, len(_rotate_chain),
823	            )
824	
825	        def _emit_raw_io(status: str, raw_response) -> None:
826	            # I-obs-001 #1141 AC3 (gate iter-1 P1): ONE raw-IO record per call tagged by its TRUE
827	            # outcome — "ok" only after a parsed verdict; "judge_error" on parse-failure / fail-open.
828	            try:
829	                _io_sink = _orc.current_raw_io_sink()
830	                if _io_sink is None:
831	                    return
832	                import uuid as _uuid
833	                _io_sink.record(
834	                    call_id=_uuid.uuid4().hex, call_type="nli_conflict_judge", role="evaluator",
835	                    request={**json_body, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]},
836	                    raw_response=raw_response, duration_ms=None, status=status,
837	                )
838	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
839	                pass
840	
841	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import BudgetExceededError
842	
843	        def _post_once() -> dict:
844	            """ONE real POST + cost-ledger + budget check. Returns the raw provider JSON.
845	
846	            Billed per ACTUAL call (each B14 retry that re-invokes this is a real call), so
847	            the cost ledger stays honest. ``BudgetExceededError`` propagates unchanged (the
848	            B14 guard re-raises it via ``propagate``) so the caller's keep-partial fires."""
849	            # I-wire-008 (#1322): the B14 guard calls this once per attempt; on every re-attempt
850	            # (calls>0) ROTATE the pinned provider to the next healthy host BEFORE the POST, so a
851	            # no-choices/blank lead is skipped instead of re-hammered. First call keeps the lead.
852	            _provider_cursor["calls"] += 1
853	            if _provider_cursor["calls"] > 1:
854	                _rotate_provider()
855	            # I-arch-007 BUG-2 (verify-hang): HARD total wall-deadline around the POST so a trickled
856	            # keep-alive socket (the per-read gap timer never fires) cannot hang the verify phase. On
857	            # timeout: force-close + rebuild a fresh client, then RAISE — the bare raise propagates up to
858	            # `call_side_judge_with_guard`, which treats ANY non-`propagate` exception as a failed attempt
859	            # and (after its bounded retry) emits the existing conflict_unscored / fail-open outcome. The
860	            # (label, confidence) verdict logic + strict-fail-closed contract are UNCHANGED.
861	            try:
862	                response = _post_with_total_deadline(
863	                    self._client,
864	                    self._endpoint,
865	                    {
866	                        "Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}",
867	                        "Content-Type": "application/json",
868	                    },
869	                    json_body,
870	                    _JUDGE_TOTAL_S,
871	                )
872	            except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:
873	                # SINGLE-WRITER safe (no thread-local needed, unlike entailment_judge.py:340-351): this
874	                # judge runs in the SEQUENTIAL P5 claim-graph step (credibility_pass._run_chain -> build_
875	                # claim_graph -> detect_semantic_conflicts), which judges pairs SERIALLY on the main thread
876	                # — NOT inside a verifier ThreadPoolExecutor fan-out (that is the EARLIER, separate P3 step
877	                # in run_credibility_analysis). So no sibling worker is ever mid-`post` on this shared client
878	                # when the force-close fires -> the Q78 cross-thread-close TLS race cannot occur here.
879	                self._client = self._build_client()  # old one was force-closed in the helper
880	                raise RuntimeError(
881	                    f"nli_conflict_total_deadline_exceeded_{int(_JUDGE_TOTAL_S)}s"
882	                )
883	            response.raise_for_status()
884	            served = response.json()
885	            usage = served.get("usage", {}) or {}
886	            input_tokens = int(usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
887	            output_tokens = int(usage.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
888	            api_cost = float(usage.get("cost", 0) or 0)
889	            actual_cost = api_cost or _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
890	                self._model, input_tokens, output_tokens, 0,
891	            )
892	            # I-wire-008 (#1322): NARROW the phantom impute to the ambiguous "content present, usage
893	            # missing" case. A no-choices/error-body-200 is a ZERO-completion transport fault; billing
894	            # it the phantom 400/60 on every rotation attempt during a provider-health storm would
895	            # inflate the run budget on zero real work (and could trip BudgetExceededError). Bill the
896	            # real (zero) usage for that attempt; the guard retries -> rotation skips the flaky host.
897	            if actual_cost == 0 and not usage and _no_choices_reason(served) is None:
898	                actual_cost = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(self._model, 400, 60, 0)
899	            _orc._add_run_cost(actual_cost)
900	            # FX-11b (#1117): also write the canonical cost-ledger ROW for this
901	            # NLI-conflict judge call. It already feeds the run BUDGET via
902	            # _add_run_cost above, but without a ledger row the persisted ledger
903	            # total trails the run-budget total whenever PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT is
904	            # on. Route through the same canonical writer + ambient-run-id key the
905	            # judge/role writers use, so all rows of one run share one accumulator.
906	            # append_cost_ledger_row bumps the SEPARATE ledger accumulator (not
907	            # _RUN_COST_CTX), so this does NOT double-count the run budget.
908	            # FX-11c (#1136): write the row BEFORE check_run_budget — a
909	            # budget-BREACHING call is already billed to the accumulator by
910	            # _add_run_cost, so it must also land in the ledger; if check_run_budget
911	            # raised first the breaching call would be billed-but-unledgered (the
912	            # exact ledger<budget drift FX-11/FX-11b exist to eliminate).
913	            try:
914	                _orc.append_cost_ledger_row(
915	                    session_id=_orc.current_run_id() or "",
916	                    call_type="nli_conflict_judge",
917	                    cost_usd=actual_cost,
918	                    input_tokens=input_tokens,
919	                    output_tokens=output_tokens,
920	                )
921	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — ledger I/O must never abort detection
922	                pass
923	            _orc.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if cap breached
924	            return served
925	
926	        # I-arch-005 B13 (#1257): route the call through the B14 empty-content guard so a
927	        # GLM-5.1 empty/None ``message.content`` (the reasoning-model collapse class) is
928	        # RETRIED before it ever reaches ``json.loads(None)``. On a NON-empty response the
929	        # guard returns it on the first attempt (byte-equivalent to the old single POST for
930	        # the happy path); on PERSISTENT empty it returns a ``JudgeUnavailable`` sentinel
931	        # (it does NOT raise) which we translate to the ``CONFLICT_UNSCORED_LABEL`` so the
932	        # pair is LABELED ``conflict_unscored`` (disclosed gap) rather than HELD. The guard
933	        # captures every attempt's raw request/response to the active raw-IO sink (the
934	        # empty-content upstream cause is confirmed, not inferred). ``BudgetExceededError``
935	        # is re-raised unchanged via ``propagate`` so keep-partial still fires.
936	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.side_judge_guard import (  # noqa: PLC0415
937	            call_side_judge_with_guard,
938	        )
939	
940	        data = None  # I-obs-001 #1141 AC3: bound before the try so the fail-open capture can prefer
941	        # the served response (post ok, parse failed) over the exception string.
942	        try:
943	            _guard = call_side_judge_with_guard(
944	                _post_once,
945	                extract_content=lambda r: r["choices"][0]["message"]["content"],
946	                call_type="nli_conflict_judge",
947	                role="evaluator",
948	                build_request=lambda: {
949	                    **json_body, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
950	                },
951	                propagate=(BudgetExceededError,),
952	            )
953	            if _guard.is_unavailable:
954	                # PERSISTENT empty content after the B14 retry → LABEL the pair
955	                # conflict_unscored, never HOLD (operator-locked "nothing shall hold the
956	                # report"). This is a disclosed gap: no conflict asserted, none dropped — the
957	                # judge could not adjudicate. Strict-fail-closed does NOT raise here: the B13
958	                # contract is that an EMPTY judge is always a LABEL, never a hold.
959	                logger.warning(
960	                    "[semantic-conflict] judge content empty after retry -> "
961	                    "conflict_unscored LABEL (could not adjudicate): %s",
962	                    _guard.unavailable.reason if _guard.unavailable else "",
963	                )
964	                return CONFLICT_UNSCORED_LABEL, 0.0
965	            data = _guard.value
966	            content = data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
967	            # I-arch-011 (B12): brace-aware first-complete-JSON-object extractor instead of a
968	            # bare json.loads(content). A "garbled-200" (valid verdict object + trailing
969	            # reasoning text) raised JSONDecodeError "Extra data: ..." and was treated as a
970	            # parse failure (HOLD under strict gates / fail-open neutral otherwise), dropping a
971	            # salvageable verdict. The extractor RAISES on every genuine failure
972	            # (empty/None/lone-"{"/partial JSON), so those still hit the same fail-closed
973	            # except-block below — only the object-plus-trailing-text case is newly rescued.
974	            parsed = _extract_first_json_object(content)
975	            verdict = str(parsed.get("verdict", "")).strip().upper()
976	            # Codex iter-3 P1 (fail-closed): a parsed-but-INVALID verdict string must NOT silently
977	            # map to "neutral" via a .get(...) default. With the broadened parser more bodies now
978	            # parse, so an out-of-vocabulary verdict (e.g. "CONFLICT", "UNSURE", "") would otherwise
979	            # become a fail-OPEN neutral under strict gates. Raise -> the except below routes it to
980	            # the SAME strict-HOLD / fail-closed path as a parse failure.
981	            _ALLOWED_VERDICTS = {"CONTRADICT", "ENTAIL", "NEUTRAL"}
982	            if verdict not in _ALLOWED_VERDICTS:
983	                raise ValueError(f"invalid semantic-conflict verdict: {verdict!r}")
984	            confidence = float(parsed.get("confidence", 0.0) or 0.0)
985	            label = {"CONTRADICT": "contradict", "ENTAIL": "entail",
986	                     "NEUTRAL": "neutral"}[verdict]
987	            # Parsed verdict → the ONE success raw-IO record, AFTER parse (gate iter-1 P1).
988	            _emit_raw_io("ok", data)
989	            return label, confidence
990	        except Exception:
991	            # BudgetExceededError is a subclass-free RuntimeError in openrouter_client;
992	            # re-raise it explicitly so the caller's keep-partial path fires.
993	            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import BudgetExceededError
994	            import sys
995	            exc = sys.exc_info()[1]
996	            if isinstance(exc, BudgetExceededError):
997	                raise
998	            # I-arch-004 F07 (#1249/#1252): under strict gates, a transport/parse failure must
999	            # FAIL CLOSED — RAISE so detect_semantic_conflicts converts it to a run-level HOLD
1000	            # (ConflictJudgeUnavailableError) instead of returning the fail-open ('neutral',
1001	            # 0.0) that silently drops a possible real contradiction. NO conflict is fabricated:
1002	            # the raise means "could not adjudicate", not "a conflict exists".
1003	            if self._strict_fail_closed:
1004	                logger.warning(
1005	                    "[semantic-conflict] judge call failed under strict gates -> "
1006	                    "FAIL CLOSED (run holds for review): %s", exc,
1007	                )
1008	                raise
1009	            logger.warning("[semantic-conflict] judge call failed (fail-open neutral): %s", exc)
1010	            # I-obs-001 #1141 AC3: capture the fail-OPEN judge_error — like the entailment judge, this
1011	            # silently drops a possible real conflict on a transient/parse failure (drb_72-class
1012	            # signal). Prefer the bound served `data`. Default-OFF; never raises.
1013	            try:
1014	                _io_sink = _orc.current_raw_io_sink()
1015	                if _io_sink is not None:
1016	                    import uuid as _uuid
1017	                    _io_sink.record(
1018	                        call_id=_uuid.uuid4().hex, call_type="nli_conflict_judge", role="evaluator",
1019	                        request={**json_body, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]},
1020	                        raw_response=(data if data is not None else {"error": str(exc)}),
1021	                        duration_ms=None, status="judge_error",
1022	                    )
1023	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
1024	                pass
1025	            return "neutral", 0.0
1026	
1027	
1028	_JUDGE_SINGLETON = None
1029	
1030	
1031	def get_default_judge(*, strict_fail_closed: bool = False):
1032	    """Lazy singleton production judge callable ``(a, b) -> (label, confidence)``.
1033	
1034	    Constructed only when ``PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT`` is ON and first used — off-mode
1035	    never instantiates it (no network, no httpx client).
1036	
1037	    I-arch-004 F07 (#1249/#1252): ``strict_fail_closed`` propagates to the judge so a
1038	    transport/parse error RAISES (fail-closed hold) instead of the additive fail-open
1039	    ('neutral', 0.0). The singleton is keyed on the flag so a strict caller never reuses
1040	    a non-strict judge instance (and vice-versa)."""
1041	    global _JUDGE_SINGLETON
1042	    if _JUDGE_SINGLETON is None or getattr(
1043	        _JUDGE_SINGLETON, "_strict_fail_closed", False
1044	    ) != bool(strict_fail_closed):
1045	        _JUDGE_SINGLETON = _SemanticContradictionJudge(strict_fail_closed=strict_fail_closed)
1046	    return _JUDGE_SINGLETON.judge
1047

1	"""I-cap-002 feature 1/4 (#1060): extract STORM perspective questions as benchmark search queries.
2	
3	STORM (`agents/storm_interviews.run_storm_interviews`) produces multi-perspective interview rounds.
4	For the benchmark we use ONLY the QUESTION text of each round as an additional search query — the
5	serialized conversation rounds do not persist the per-question decomposed `search_queries`, so the
6	contract is questions-only. STORM never produces evidence (`direct_quote`) here; it only widens the
7	search-query fan-out, so the verbatim-span faithfulness path (live_retriever -> strict_verify) is
8	untouched.
9	"""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	from typing import Any
14	
15	
16	def extract_storm_questions(
17	    storm_conversations: list[dict[str, Any]] | None,
18	    cap: int = 30,
19	) -> list[str]:
20	    """Flatten STORM conversation rounds into a deduplicated, bounded list of question strings.
21	
22	    Order-preserving, case-insensitive de-duplication. Returns at most ``cap`` questions. Robust to
23	    missing/empty keys (returns ``[]`` rather than raising).
24	    """
25	    if cap <= 0:
26	        return []
27	    seen: set[str] = set()
28	    out: list[str] = []
29	    for conversation in storm_conversations or []:
30	        if not isinstance(conversation, dict):
31	            continue
32	        for round_ in conversation.get("rounds", []) or []:
33	            if not isinstance(round_, dict):
34	                continue
35	            # Coerce to str defensively — a malformed {"question": 123} must not raise.
36	            question = str(round_.get("question") or "").strip()
37	            if not question:
38	                continue
39	            key = question.lower()
40	            if key in seen:
41	                continue
42	            seen.add(key)
43	            out.append(question)
44	            if len(out) >= cap:
45	                return out
46	    return out
47

1	"""R2 entity-qgen (I-deepfix-001, #1344) — STORM-style sub-entity + perspective query expansion.
2	
3	THE GAP (measured, drb_72 AI-labor SWEEP): the FS-Researcher / expert-facet frontier decomposes the
4	question into ABSTRACT facets (sub-topics x mechanism/stakeholder/counter/temporal/geographic angles).
5	That surfaces the famous canonical studies (Acemoglu / Autor / Frey-Osborne) but MISSES the long-tail
6	NICHE sub-entities a DRB-II rubric actually names — the concrete occupations, industry sectors, and
7	demographic groups whose profession-specific primaries never appear in a generic "AI + labor market"
8	search. Abstract facets do not name "radiologists", "paralegals", "truck drivers", "warehouse
9	workers" — so their profession-specific evidence is never fetched, and the corpus stays canonical-only.
10	
11	THE FIX (STORM-style, arXiv:2402.14207 multi-perspective + per-sub-entity decomposition): ONE bounded
12	LLM call enumerates the concrete NAMED sub-entities the question implies (occupations / sectors /
13	demographic groups / named technologies-datasets), and a DETERMINISTIC set of STORM-style disciplinary
14	PERSPECTIVE lenses (economic / social / policy / industry / technical / public-interest) multiplies the
15	topic at $0. Every emitted query carries the question's own subject keywords as a scope anchor (the same
16	``distill_keywords`` anchor R1 uses), so a sub-entity query can never generalise off-subject (the
17	drb_72-v2 off-topic drift guard). This AUGMENTS the FS-Researcher / expert-facet frontier — it does not
18	replace it.
19	
20	DNA (§-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE): pure query-FRONTIER expansion. It ADDS on-topic queries only; it
21	DROPS ZERO sources; every discovered source flows through the UNCHANGED ``per_query_retrieve`` (scope
22	gate -> tier classify -> fetch -> provenance) and the frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI /
23	4-role / span-grounding) exactly as today — a niche sub-entity source is NEVER auto-trusted, it must
24	earn its citation like every other source. ``PG_SUBENTITY_MAX_ENTITIES`` / ``PG_SUBENTITY_MAX_PERSPECTIVES``
25	and the FS-Researcher ``_max_queries`` cap are compute-SAFETY bounds (they cap cost — the opposite of the
26	banned pattern, which forces a breadth number UP); no forced cap / target / thinner / breadth-canary is
27	introduced. Default OFF (``PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION``) => the FS-Researcher path is byte-identical.
28	"""
29	
30	from __future__ import annotations
31	
32	import os
33	import re
34	from typing import Callable
35	
36	# (prompt) -> text. The already-picked FS-Researcher / intent-frame policy model, injected by the
37	# caller (async client wrapped to sync) so this module never imports a live client at load time.
38	LlmFn = Callable[[str], str]
39	
40	# STORM-style disciplinary PERSPECTIVE lenses (arXiv:2402.14207 personas), TOPIC-AGNOSTIC: each lens is
41	# a short disciplinary-vantage phrase spliced alongside the question's own subject keywords (the scope
42	# anchor). Deterministic — one bounded LLM call enumerates the named sub-entities; these lenses multiply
43	# the topic at $0. Distinct from R1's analytical angle lenses (mechanism/stakeholder/counter/temporal/
44	# geographic): these are DISCIPLINARY personas, so they widen toward vantage points R1 does not cover.
45	# The order is stable so the emitted frontier is reproducible.
46	_PERSPECTIVE_LENSES: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
47	    ("economic", "economic analysis costs benefits"),
48	    ("social", "social workforce community impact"),
49	    ("policy", "policy regulation governance response"),
50	    ("industry", "industry practitioner adoption deployment"),
51	    ("technical", "technical mechanism feasibility limits"),
52	    ("public_interest", "public interest affected groups equity"),
53	)
54	
55	
56	def sub_entity_expansion_enabled() -> bool:
57	    """True iff the sub-entity + perspective query expansion is flag-enabled (default OFF = legacy).
58	
59	    LAW VI env kill-switch. Default OFF keeps the FS-Researcher path byte-identical until the fresh
60	    paid run that validates the widened frontier; the tests exercise the ON path explicitly.
61	    """
62	    return os.getenv("PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True")
63	
64	
65	def _max_sub_entities() -> int:
66	    """Max concrete sub-entities kept from the LLM enumeration. A compute-safety CAP on cost
67	    (entities x fetch), NOT a breadth target — it bounds the frontier UP-side, never forces a
68	    number up (§-1.3). Default 12."""
69	    try:
70	        return max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_SUBENTITY_MAX_ENTITIES", "12")))
71	    except ValueError:
72	        return 12
73	
74	
75	def _max_perspectives() -> int:
76	    """How many STORM-style perspective lenses to emit (<= the number defined). Another compute-safety
77	    bound on cost, defaulting to the full lens set."""
78	    try:
79	        n = int(os.getenv("PG_SUBENTITY_MAX_PERSPECTIVES", str(len(_PERSPECTIVE_LENSES))))
80	    except ValueError:
81	        n = len(_PERSPECTIVE_LENSES)
82	    return max(1, min(n, len(_PERSPECTIVE_LENSES)))
83	
84	
85	def _sub_entity_reserve() -> int:
86	    """How many sub-entity / perspective queries are ADDED ON TOP of the full baseline query budget.
87	
88	    Without this fix, a wide R1 facet frontier fills the whole ``PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER_MAX_QUERIES``
89	    budget, so making room for a sub-entity query would have to DISPLACE a baseline facet query — a
90	    SWAP, not a widen (the Codex/Fable iter-1 REVISE). Instead, this bounded slice is added as EXTRA
91	    budget: the effective query budget is RAISED by up to this many positions so the sub-entity queries
92	    issue ON TOP of — never in place of — the baseline the flag-OFF path would issue. §-1.3
93	    WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE widen-only: a compute-safety UP-side bound on the added retrieval (LAW VI env
94	    cap), never a breadth target and never a drop. Env-driven ``PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_RESERVE`` (default 10)."""
95	    try:
96	        return max(0, int(os.getenv("PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_RESERVE", "10")))
97	    except ValueError:
98	        return 10
99	
100	
101	def _norm(text: str) -> str:
102	    """Collapse internal whitespace and trim."""
103	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", (text or "")).strip()
104	
105	
106	def _scope_anchor(question: str) -> str:
107	    """The question's own subject keywords, used verbatim as a scope anchor on EVERY emitted query.
108	
109	    Reuses ``query_decomposer.distill_keywords`` (the public, in-scope retrieval util R1's
110	    ``_question_anchor`` is built on) so the anchor is the same distilled keyword phrase the keyword
111	    backends and the sibling facet planner already use. Falls back to the question's own leading content
112	    words when distillation yields nothing (never returns an empty anchor — an unanchored sub-entity
113	    query is exactly the off-topic drift this fix must not re-create).
114	    """
115	    try:
116	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.query_decomposer import distill_keywords
117	        anchor = (distill_keywords(question, max_terms=8) or "").strip()
118	    except Exception:
119	        anchor = ""
120	    if anchor:
121	        return anchor
122	    words = [w for w in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9\-]+", question or "") if len(w) > 2]
123	    return " ".join(words[:8]).strip() or (question or "").strip()
124	
125	
126	def _clean_entity_lines(text: str, cap: int) -> list[str]:
127	    """Parse an LLM sub-entity reply into clean, de-duplicated entity names.
128	
129	    Strips numbering / bullets / a leading dimension label ("OCCUPATION:", "Sector -", ...), drops
130	    obvious preamble lines, enforces a short length floor, and de-duplicates case-insensitively.
131	    Bounded by ``cap``. Mirrors the sibling facet planner's line parsing so entity and facet parsing
132	    behave the same.
133	    """
134	    out: list[str] = []
135	    seen: set[str] = set()
136	    for raw in (text or "").splitlines():
137	        s = raw.strip()
138	        if not s:
139	            continue
140	        s = re.sub(r"^\s*(?:[-*]|\d+[.):])\s*", "", s).strip()
141	        # strip an optional leading dimension label ("OCCUPATION:", "Demographic -", ...)
142	        s = re.sub(r"^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z /_-]{2,20}\s*[:\-]\s+", "", s).strip().strip('"').strip()
143	        if len(s) <= 2:
144	            continue
145	        low = s.lower()
146	        if low.startswith(("here", "sure", "the following", "none", "sub-entit", "sub entit")):
147	            continue
148	        if low in seen:
149	            continue
150	        seen.add(low)
151	        out.append(s)
152	        if len(out) >= cap:
153	            break
154	    return out
155	
156	
157	def _enumerate_sub_entities(question: str, llm: LlmFn, cap: int) -> list[str]:
158	    """ONE bounded LLM call: enumerate the concrete NAMED sub-entities the question implies.
159	
160	    Elicits specific occupations / professions, industry sectors, demographic groups, and named
161	    technologies / datasets that a generic search would miss but that the review scope names or
162	    implies. Every entity MUST stay within the question's scope. Best-effort: any exception or empty
163	    reply yields ``[]`` and the caller simply emits the deterministic perspective queries — the
164	    faithfulness engine is never touched (this only decides WHICH queries are searched).
165	    """
166	    try:
167	        reply = llm(
168	            "You are an expert research planner. List the concrete, NAMED sub-entities this RESEARCH "
169	            "QUESTION implies but a generic search would MISS — specific occupations / professions, "
170	            "industry sectors, demographic groups, and named technologies or datasets whose "
171	            "profession-specific or sector-specific evidence is relevant. Prefer the long-tail, niche "
172	            "entities over the obvious headline ones. Every entity MUST stay within the scope of the "
173	            "question — do NOT drift into a broad field. One entity per line, no numbering.\n\n"
174	            "RESEARCH QUESTION:\n" + (question or "")
175	        )
176	    except Exception:
177	        return []
178	    return _clean_entity_lines(reply, cap)
179	
180	
181	def plan_sub_entity_queries(question: str, llm: LlmFn) -> list[str]:
182	    """Build the sub-entity + perspective query frontier for ``question``.
183	
184	    Returns an ordered, de-duplicated list of scope-anchored queries:
185	      * per-sub-entity queries (``{named sub-entity} {question anchor}``) — the niche occupation /
186	        sector / demographic primaries a generic search misses, from ONE bounded LLM call, and
187	      * STORM-style perspective queries (``{disciplinary lens} {question anchor}``) — deterministic,
188	        $0, always emitted so the frontier widens even when the LLM enumeration is empty.
189	
190	    Pure control flow over the injected ``llm`` — no network here. The FS-Researcher ``_max_queries``
191	    cap (a compute-safety bound) still applies downstream, so this may return more than are ultimately
192	    issued — intentional headroom, never a forced count (§-1.3).
193	    """
194	    anchor = _scope_anchor(question)
195	    out: list[str] = []
196	    seen: set[str] = set()
197	
198	    def _add(q: str) -> None:
199	        q = _norm(q)
200	        if not q:
201	            return
202	        key = q.lower()
203	        if key in seen:
204	            return
205	        seen.add(key)
206	        out.append(q)
207	
208	    # Per-sub-entity queries (named, niche, scope-anchored) — the core recall lever.
209	    for entity in _enumerate_sub_entities(question, llm, _max_sub_entities()):
210	        _add(f"{entity} {anchor}")
211	
212	    # STORM-style disciplinary perspective queries (deterministic floor — always widens).
213	    for _label, lens in _PERSPECTIVE_LENSES[: _max_perspectives()]:
214	        _add(f"{lens} {anchor}")
215	
216	    return out
217	
218	
219	def widen_with_sub_entities(
220	    base_seeds: list[str],
221	    sub_entity_queries: list[str],
222	    max_queries: int,
223	) -> tuple[list[str], int]:
224	    """ADD the sub-entity / perspective queries ON TOP of the full baseline frontier and RAISE the
225	    effective query budget by exactly the added slice, so the flag-ON issued query set is a strict
226	    SUPERSET of the flag-OFF issued set.
227	
228	    Returns ``(widened_seed_queries, effective_max_queries)``.
229	
230	    The flag-OFF path issues ``base_seeds[:max_queries]``. This keeps that exact baseline window at the
231	    FRONT in unchanged order, inserts a bounded slice of sub-entity queries immediately AFTER it, and
232	    returns a RAISED budget ``max_queries + reserve``. The downstream seed-issue loop (capped at the
233	    returned budget) therefore emits ``base_seeds[:max_queries] + reserved`` — every baseline query the
234	    flag-OFF path would issue is STILL issued, and the sub-entity / perspective queries are ADDED, never
235	    SWAPPED IN for a baseline query. This is the iter-1 REVISE fix: the prior helper shrank the baseline
236	    head to ``max_queries - reserve`` so the reserved slice displaced baseline queries out of the budget
237	    (a swap). Any baseline beyond the original budget and any leftover sub-entity queries trail after and
238	    issue only if the raised budget still has room — never displacing a baseline query. An empty
239	    ``sub_entity_queries`` (or a reserve of 0) returns ``(base_seeds, max_queries)`` unchanged
240	    (byte-identical no-op).
241	
242	    §-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE widen-only: this ADDS retrieval by raising the budget by the bounded
243	    reserve (a LAW VI env cap, ``PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_RESERVE``); it introduces no cap / target / thinner,
244	    drops ZERO sources, and every added query still routes through the UNCHANGED ``per_query_retrieve``
245	    (scope -> tier -> fetch -> provenance) and the frozen faithfulness engine — a sub-entity source is
246	    NEVER auto-trusted.
247	    """
248	    if not sub_entity_queries:
249	        return list(base_seeds), max_queries
250	    reserve = min(len(sub_entity_queries), _sub_entity_reserve())
251	    if reserve <= 0:
252	        return list(base_seeds), max_queries
253	    reserved = sub_entity_queries[:reserve]
254	    leftover = sub_entity_queries[reserve:]
255	    baseline_window = base_seeds[:max_queries]  # exactly what the flag-OFF path issues
256	    baseline_tail = base_seeds[max_queries:]    # baseline beyond budget (neither path issues)
257	    widened = baseline_window + reserved + baseline_tail + leftover
258	    return widened, max_queries + reserve
259

1	"""I-beatboth-001 (#1276) — fetch-shell / web-boilerplate detector (pure leaf module).
2	
3	WHY THIS EXISTS
4	---------------
5	run7 (drb_78 Parkinson's/DBS) shipped junk web-boilerplate spans as VERIFIED cited
6	clinical findings (state/beatboth_campaign/PHASE1_ISSUES.md P0-1): a 476-char CAPTCHA /
7	Cloudflare "Just a moment… Performing security verification… verifies you are not a bot"
8	page grounded 6 top-of-report units, plus cookie-consent banners, an HTTP 404, a
9	language-picker nav menu, citation-manager UI chrome ("Download Citation"/CrossRef), and
10	YouTube related-video sidebars — all rendered as corroborated findings. They passed because
11	the report prose verbatim-copies the junk span, so the numeric/content-overlap/entailment
12	checks trivially hold (the "self-citation hole"). This is the I-bug-775 fetch-shell class.
13	
14	The existing ``live_retriever._is_access_denial_stub`` / ``is_content_starved`` runs at
15	RETRIEVAL / refetch time on the full fetched body. But a shell already in the corpus (or a
16	shell reloaded UNTOUCHED on resume — resume_refetch.py STILL-SHELL) never gets re-checked at
17	the per-cited-span faithfulness gate (``verify_sentence_provenance``). This module is the
18	single source of truth for shell vocabulary so the SAME detector covers both layers (LAW V:
19	one list, no drift — the markers were historically bolted on in waves I-run11-010 then RC-C,
20	which is exactly the divergence this consolidation prevents).
21	
22	POSTURE (binding)
23	-----------------
24	* STRICTER gate only — a shell can NEVER ground a claim; it is fail-closed in
25	  ``verify_sentence_provenance``. Faithfulness is TIGHTENED, never relaxed.
26	* DETERMINISTIC + pure: no LLM, no network, no row mutation. snake_case, LAW VI env knobs.
27	* HIGH-PRECISION: every default-on marker is either a challenge-PAGE-specific phrase that
28	  never appears in real article prose, or an ALL-of co-occurrence tuple. A loose phrase that
29	  could appear in a legitimate clinical/methods/bibliography span is gated to a SHORT body
30	  (so a full article that merely mentions the word is never false-dropped) — a false-drop
31	  would itself be a §-1.3 breadth loss.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import os
37	import re
38	
39	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

41	# constants re-point here; do not redefine them there.

200	    "pàgina no trobada",  # CA: "page not found" (the run-#7 Catalan 404)
201	    "página no encontrada",  # ES: "page not found"
202	    # Language-selector / nav-only pages (a bare list of language names is not article prose).
203	    "select your language",
204	    "choose your language",
205	    # Subscription / paywall interstitials with no body.
206	    "subscribe to continue reading",
207	    "to continue reading this article",
208	    "create a free account to continue",
209	    # I-fetchclean-001 (2026-07-10) — the exact replay error_page marker; short-body
210	    # gated so a long article that merely quotes the phrase is never false-dropped.
211	    "something went wrong. wait a moment",
212	    # I-fetchclean-001 round-1 (2026-07-10) — additional bot-wall / transient-error stub
213	    # copy (ev_688 UQ wall short-body form + generic transient-error interstitial). Each
214	    # is page-chrome that never constitutes a clinical claim; short-body-gated so a long
215	    # article that merely quotes the phrase is never false-dropped.
216	    "let's confirm you are human",
217	    "temporary error. please try again",
218	)

220	# I-beatboth-001 iter-2 (Codex P1 #2): the subset of single phrases that are NOT shell-specific
221	# enough to fail-close a cited span on their own. Each of these can appear ONCE inside a
222	# legitimate short abstract / methods / nav-bearing real page, so the cited-span gate requires
223	# them to be CORROBORATED — fire only when (a) ANOTHER independent shell signal co-occurs
224	# (multi-signal), OR (b) the body is VERY short (a bare stub page, governed by a much tighter
225	# ceiling than the 3000-char one used for the unambiguous strong phrases). A real 1500-char
226	# abstract that says "access denied" once trips neither clause and passes. Drawn from
227	# ACCESS_DENIAL_MARKERS / SHORT_BODY_SHELL_MARKERS — this is a VIEW for the cited-span gate's
228	# precision logic, NOT a separate vocabulary (LAW V: the strings still live in the shared tuples,
229	# so the retrieval-time ``is_access_denial_stub`` stays byte-identical).
230	AMBIGUOUS_SHELL_PHRASES: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
231	    {
232	        "access denied",  # legit: "access denied to the dataset was reported in 12% of cases"
233	        "security verification",  # legit: a methods span describing outcome/security verification
234	        "this process is automatic",  # legit: prose describing an automated clinical workflow
235	        "change language",  # legit: nav chrome word; appears in real multilingual pages
236	    }
237	)

239	# LAW VI — env knobs, no magic numbers.
240	# Master kill-switch for the CITED-SPAN shell gate in verify_sentence_provenance. Default ON
241	# (this is a faithfulness-TIGHTENING gate); set PG_CITED_SPAN_SHELL_DETECT=0 to revert to the
242	# byte-identical pre-#1276 behaviour (a shell span verifies on numeric/content overlap as before).
243	_ENV_ENABLED = "PG_CITED_SPAN_SHELL_DETECT"
244	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", "disabled"})
245	
246	# Max body length at which a STRONG (unambiguous) short-body single-phrase marker may fire (the
247	# bot-wall + http-error + nav classes). A real article body is far longer; this guards against
248	# false-dropping a full article that merely quotes one phrase. Shared default with live_retriever's
249	# prior 3000.
250	_ENV_SHORT_BODY_MAX = "PG_SHELL_SHORT_BODY_MAX_CHARS"
251	_DEFAULT_SHORT_BODY_MAX = 3000
252	
253	# I-beatboth-001 iter-2 — chrome ceiling for the SHELL_COOCCURRENCE classes (cookie / citation-UI /
254	# social). A genuine chrome shell is SHORT — a near-empty page whose whole body IS the widget
255	# (verified on the real drb_78 corpus: the genuine chrome shells are <=1500 chars, while the rows
256	# >800 carrying the same vocab are real articles with an incidental footer). A chrome class fires
257	# ONLY when the body is at or under this ceiling; above it the class does NOT fire and the long
258	# real article passes (no pure-string test can safely re-fire these on a long body — see the
259	# SHELL_COOCCURRENCE comment). This is a plain length gate, deliberately NOT a "dominance ratio":
260	# the ratio approach was dead code (matched-marker length can never reach 50% of an >800-char body)
261	# AND, per Codex P1 #1, no length-agnostic firing of these classes is safe.
262	_ENV_CHROME_MAX = "PG_SHELL_CHROME_MAX_CHARS"
263	_DEFAULT_CHROME_MAX = 800
264	
265	# I-beatboth-001 iter-2 — the much-tighter ceiling under which a single AMBIGUOUS phrase may fire
266	# WITHOUT a corroborating second signal (a bare "Access Denied" stub page is tiny). A real
267	# abstract that says "access denied" once is well above this, so it is never false-dropped on a
268	# single ambiguous phrase.
269	_ENV_AMBIGUOUS_SHORT_BODY_MAX = "PG_SHELL_AMBIGUOUS_SHORT_BODY_MAX_CHARS"
270	_DEFAULT_AMBIGUOUS_SHORT_BODY_MAX = 200

282	def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
283	    """Read a positive int env knob (LAW VI). A bad/empty/non-positive value falls back to the
284	    safe default — never an unbounded ceiling that would let a long shell through, never a tiny
285	    one that would over-fire on a clinical abstract."""
286	    try:
287	        value = int(os.environ.get(name, default) or default)
288	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
289	        return default
290	    return value if value > 0 else default

309	def is_access_denial_stub(content: str, *, max_chars: int | None = None) -> bool:
310	    """True if ``content`` looks like a bot-challenge / access-denial page rather than article
311	    content. Mirrors the prior ``live_retriever._is_access_denial_stub`` byte-for-byte (the
312	    short-body markers fire only on a short body; the unambiguous Cloudflare co-occurrence
313	    signatures fire at any length), so re-pointing live_retriever here is behaviour-preserving.
314	    """
315	    if not content:
316	        return False
317	    low = content.lower()
318	    if any(all(tok in low for tok in combo) for combo in CHALLENGE_PAGE_COOCCURRENCE):
319	        return True
320	    ceiling = max_chars if max_chars is not None else _short_body_max_chars()
321	    if len(content.strip()) > ceiling:
322	        return False
323	    return any(marker in low for marker in ACCESS_DENIAL_MARKERS)
324	
325	
326	def _chrome_class_present(low: str, body_len: int) -> bool:
327	    """I-beatboth-001 iter-2 (Codex P1 #1) — True iff a SHELL_COOCCURRENCE chrome class is present
328	    AND the body is SHORT enough to be the chrome page itself. A genuine cookie / citation-UI /
329	    social chrome shell is a near-empty page whose whole body IS the widget; a long real article
330	    merely carries an incidental footer. Per Codex's finding these chrome phrases DO appear in long
331	    real article bodies, so there is no safe length-agnostic firing — the class fires ONLY on a
332	    short body. Above the ceiling the long real article passes (no false-drop = no §-1.3 breadth
333	    loss). This is a plain length gate, NOT a ratio: a ratio of matched-marker length to body length
334	    can never exceed 50% of an >800-char body (dead code), and no string ratio can distinguish a
335	    verbose all-chrome page from a long article discussing cookie policy — that needs the
336	    content/NLI classification the pipeline already applies at other layers."""
337	    if body_len > _chrome_max_chars():
338	        return False
339	    return any(all(tok in low for tok in combo) for combo in SHELL_COOCCURRENCE)
340	
341	
342	def _ambiguous_phrase_fires(low: str, body_len: int) -> bool:
343	    """I-beatboth-001 iter-2 (Codex P1 #2) — True iff an AMBIGUOUS single phrase ("access denied",
344	    "security verification", "this process is automatic", "change language") may fail-close a cited
345	    span. These are NOT shell-specific enough alone, so they require CORROBORATION: fire only when
346	    (a) a SECOND independent shell signal co-occurs (multi-signal), OR (b) the body is VERY short (a
347	    bare stub page, a much tighter ceiling than the strong-phrase 3000). A real abstract that says
348	    "access denied" once is above the tight ceiling and carries no second signal, so it passes."""
349	    present = [phrase for phrase in AMBIGUOUS_SHELL_PHRASES if phrase in low]
350	    if not present:
351	        return False
352	    # (b) bare stub page — very short body, a single ambiguous phrase is enough.
353	    if body_len <= _ambiguous_short_body_max_chars():
354	        return True
355	    # (a) multi-signal — a SECOND, independent shell signal must co-occur, AND the body must be
356	    # SHORT. Every corroboration branch is bounded by ``_short_body_max_chars()`` for the SAME reason
357	    # as the main path (Codex P1 #1): otherwise a long real article that happens to carry two
358	    # ambiguous phrases (plausible once generalized off-clinical: a security/IT/systems-methods paper
359	    # using "access denied" + "security verification"), or one ambiguous phrase plus an incidental
360	    # cookie footer, would be false-dropped here at ANY length — re-opening exactly the finding the
361	    # main path closed. A genuine corroborated stub is short; a long real article is not.
362	    if body_len > _short_body_max_chars():
363	        return False
364	    # second signal: another ambiguous phrase, a strong access-denial marker, an HTTP/nav marker, or
365	    # a (short-body) chrome class. A lone ambiguous phrase in a real short abstract trips none of it.
366	    if len(present) >= 2:
367	        return True
368	    strong_denial = [m for m in ACCESS_DENIAL_MARKERS if m not in AMBIGUOUS_SHELL_PHRASES]
369	    if any(marker in low for marker in strong_denial):
370	        return True
371	    if any(marker in low for marker in SHORT_BODY_SHELL_MARKERS):
372	        return True
373	    if _chrome_class_present(low, body_len):
374	        return True
375	    return False
376

378	def is_cited_span_shell(direct_quote: str) -> bool:
379	    """I-beatboth-001 (#1276) — True iff the cited source body is a fetch-shell / web-boilerplate
380	    page that can NEVER ground a clinical claim (CAPTCHA / security-verification interstitial,
381	    cookie-consent, HTTP 404/403, language-nav, citation-UI chrome, social-media boilerplate).
382	
383	    DETERMINISTIC + HIGH-PRECISION (iter-2 hardening, Codex 2×P1):
384	      * The unambiguous any-length signatures — ``CHALLENGE_PAGE_COOCCURRENCE`` (bot-wall, shared
385	        with the retrieval gate) and ``CITED_SPAN_ANY_LENGTH_COOCCURRENCE`` (crawler HTTP-error
386	        wrapper, cited-span-only) — NEVER occur in real article prose, so they fire at ANY length
387	        (the 476-char run-#7 CAPTCHA span and the 6639-char conference-404 shell are both caught).
388	      * The chrome ``SHELL_COOCCURRENCE`` classes (cookie / citation-UI / social) fire ONLY on a
389	        SHORT body (``_chrome_class_present``). Per Codex P1 #1 these phrases DO appear in long real
390	        article bodies (the drb_78 corpus has 4 such genuine articles), so an incidental cookie
391	        footer / citation widget inside a long real article is NOT a shell and passes.
392	      * STRONG single-phrase markers (unambiguous bot-wall / http-error / nav / paywall) fire on a
393	        SHORT body. AMBIGUOUS phrases ("access denied", "security verification", "this process is
394	        automatic", "change language") require CORROBORATION — a second shell signal, or a
395	        much-shorter body — so a legit short abstract mentioning one of them once is NOT dropped
396	        (Codex P1 #2).
397	
398	    A false-drop is itself a §-1.3 breadth loss, so the bias is toward PASS unless the body is
399	    clearly a shell. Used fail-closed in ``verify_sentence_provenance`` on each cited token's source
400	    ``direct_quote`` — a token whose source is a shell fails exactly like ``span_out_of_bounds``, so
401	    the sentence drops and the shell can never ride on a real co-token. This propagates to every
402	    render path (the SUPPORTS-only enrichment surfacing, ``verified_support_origin_count``,
403	    ``basket_verdict``) through the existing verify wiring; no separate render filter is needed.
404	    """
405	    if not direct_quote:
406	        return False
407	    low = direct_quote.lower()
408	    body_len = len(direct_quote.strip())
409	    # ANY-length unambiguous signatures: bot-wall challenge pages (shared with the retrieval gate)
410	    # + the cited-span-only crawler HTTP-error wrappers (never appear in real article prose).
411	    for combo in (*CHALLENGE_PAGE_COOCCURRENCE, *CITED_SPAN_ANY_LENGTH_COOCCURRENCE):
412	        if all(tok in low for tok in combo):
413	            return True
414	    # Chrome co-occurrence classes (cookie / citation-UI / social) — SHORT-body only (Codex P1 #1):
415	    # a long real article carrying the same chrome vocab as an incidental footer is NOT a shell.
416	    if _chrome_class_present(low, body_len):
417	        return True
418	    # STRONG short-body single-phrase markers (bot-wall, http-error, nav, paywall): fire only on a
419	    # short body so a real article is never false-dropped on a single incidental phrase. AMBIGUOUS
420	    # phrases are EXCLUDED here — they go through the corroboration path below.
421	    if body_len <= _short_body_max_chars():
422	        strong_denial = [m for m in ACCESS_DENIAL_MARKERS if m not in AMBIGUOUS_SHELL_PHRASES]
423	        if any(marker in low for marker in strong_denial):
424	            return True
425	        if any(marker in low for marker in SHORT_BODY_SHELL_MARKERS):
426	            return True
427	    # AMBIGUOUS single phrases — corroborated multi-signal OR very-short-body only (Codex P1 #2).
428	    if _ambiguous_phrase_fires(low, body_len):
429	        return True
430	    return False

433	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
434	# I-deepfix-001 B1 (wave-2, 2026-07-08) — extraction-time FURNITURE-DENSITY screen.
435	#
436	# WHY (separate from the bot-wall gates above): the short-body shell gates catch a
437	# page whose WHOLE body is a challenge / error / cookie stub. They do NOT catch a
438	# DEGRADED PDF extraction whose FULL body is masthead / nav / DOI / license / author-
439	# affiliation FURNITURE welded together (the mineru-timeout -> docling-skip-on->40pg
440	# -> PyMuPDF-flat-text path on a big PDF). That furniture becomes the claim-local
441	# direct_quote, survives strict_verify via the self-citation hole, and forms an all-
442	# chrome basket -> writer skip -> K-span (real papers lost: Felten "Occupational
443	# Heterogeneity", a DSpace paper, World Bank). The §-1.3-safe fix is to catch a
444	# furniture-DOMINANT full body at extraction time -> mark fetch-DEGRADED -> the caller
445	# re-fetches with a DIFFERENT extractor to RECOVER the real article; if it is still
446	# furniture, down-weight + disclose (kept in the pool, NEVER hard-dropped).
447	#
448	# The per-segment furniture verdict REUSES the ONE shared render-side predicate
449	# ``weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable`` (READ-ONLY, LAZY import,
450	# fail-open) — it is never forked here (LAW V). All three B1 behaviours sit behind
451	# their own default-OFF flags; OFF => byte-identical (these functions are never called
452	# on the hot path).
453	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
454	
455	_ENV_FURNITURE_DENSITY_SCREEN = "PG_FURNITURE_DENSITY_SCREEN"
456	_ENV_FURNITURE_REFETCH = "PG_FURNITURE_REFETCH"
457	_ENV_SPAN_SELECT_FURNITURE_AWARE = "PG_SPAN_SELECT_FURNITURE_AWARE"
458	
459	# Fraction of body CHARACTERS that must sit in furniture segments for the body to
460	# count as furniture-DOMINANT. High (0.6) so a real article carrying an incidental
461	# masthead / license footer is NEVER flagged — only a body that is MOSTLY furniture.
462	_ENV_FURNITURE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD = "PG_FURNITURE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD"
463	_DEFAULT_FURNITURE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.6
464	
465	# The density verdict is meaningless on a tiny body (the short-body shell gates above
466	# already own stubs), so the screen only runs at/above this length — it targets the
467	# degraded-big-PDF furniture case, not a small stub.
468	_ENV_FURNITURE_MIN_BODY_CHARS = "PG_FURNITURE_MIN_BODY_CHARS"
469	_DEFAULT_FURNITURE_MIN_BODY_CHARS = 1200
470	
471

475	    byte-identical. Read at call time so tests toggle without re-import."""
476	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_FURNITURE_DENSITY_SCREEN, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

491	def _furniture_density_threshold() -> float:
492	    """Char-fraction ceiling above which a body is furniture-dominant. A bad/empty
493	    value falls back to the safe default; a value outside (0, 1] also falls back so a
494	    misconfig can never make every body (>=0) or no body (>1) dominant."""
495	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_FURNITURE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD, "").strip()
496	    if not raw:
497	        return _DEFAULT_FURNITURE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD
498	    try:
499	        value = float(raw)
500	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
501	        return _DEFAULT_FURNITURE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD
502	    return value if 0.0 < value <= 1.0 else _DEFAULT_FURNITURE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD
503

510	def _segment_body(body: str) -> "list[str]":
511	    """Split a fetched body into candidate segments for the furniture screen —
512	    blank-line-delimited paragraphs, falling back to single lines when the body has
513	    no blank-line structure (flat PyMuPDF text). Pure, deterministic."""
514	    if not body:
515	        return []
516	    blocks = [b for b in re.split(r"\n\s*\n", body) if b.strip()]
517	    if len(blocks) >= 2:
518	        return blocks
519	    return [ln for ln in body.splitlines() if ln.strip()]
520	
521	
522	def _is_furniture_segment(segment: str) -> bool:
523	    """READ-ONLY reuse of the shared render-side chrome/furniture predicate
524	    ``weighted_enrichment.is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable`` (LAZY import so this
525	    leaf module keeps no import-time dependency on the generator package). Fail-open
526	    ``False`` on any import/predicate error — a screen failure must never invent a
527	    furniture flag that would (downstream) degrade a real source."""
528	    seg = segment.strip()
529	    if not seg:
530	        return False
531	    try:
532	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (
533	            is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable,
534	        )
535	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — no predicate => no furniture verdict (fail-open)
536	        return False
537	    try:
538	        return bool(is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable(seg))
539	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — predicate error must never break extraction
540	        return False
541	
542	
543	def furniture_density(body: str) -> float:
544	    """Fraction of body CHARACTERS that sit in furniture segments (0.0–1.0). Pure;
545	    ``0.0`` on an empty body. CHAR-weighted so a few long real paragraphs outweigh
546	    many short furniture lines (and a wall of furniture outweighs one real line)."""
547	    if not body or not body.strip():
548	        return 0.0
549	    total = 0
550	    furniture = 0
551	    for seg in _segment_body(body):
552	        n = len(seg.strip())
553	        if n == 0:
554	            continue
555	        total += n
556	        if _is_furniture_segment(seg):
557	            furniture += n
558	    if total == 0:
559	        return 0.0
560	    return furniture / total
561	
562	
563	def is_furniture_dominant(body: str) -> bool:
564	    """True iff ``body`` is a furniture-DOMINANT degraded extraction: chrome density
565	    at/above ``PG_FURNITURE_DENSITY_THRESHOLD`` AND long enough to be the big-PDF
566	    furniture case (not a tiny stub the short-body shell gates already own). Pure,
567	    fail-open ``False``. NEVER used to DROP — only to mark a fetch degraded so the
568	    caller re-fetches / down-weights + discloses (§-1.3 RECOVER, never hard-drop)."""
569	    if not body:
570	        return False
571	    if len(body.strip()) < _furniture_min_body_chars():
572	        return False
573	    return furniture_density(body) >= _furniture_density_threshold()
574
