576	def select_real_content_span(candidate_spans: "list[str]") -> "tuple[int, str]":
577	    """I-deepfix-001 B1 step 3 — furniture-aware direct_quote span picker.
578	
579	    Given ordered candidate spans (best-first by the caller's ranking), return
580	    ``(index, span)`` of the FIRST non-furniture span so a real-content span wins the
581	    direct_quote over a furniture span. If EVERY candidate is furniture, return the
582	    FIRST non-falsy span paired with ITS REAL index — never drop (the all-furniture case
583	    is owned by the down-weight/disclose path, not this picker). Pure; the caller gates
584	    on ``span_select_furniture_aware_enabled()`` so OFF => the caller keeps its legacy
585	    span => byte-identical. Provided for the downstream direct_quote selection seam
586	    (frame_fetcher / live_retriever); this module never wires it itself.
587	
588	    INDEX CONTRACT (Fable P2a + Codex/Fable P2 gate-fix): the returned index is ALWAYS
589	    into the ORIGINAL, UNFILTERED ``candidate_spans`` list AND is SELF-CONSISTENT with
590	    the returned span — ``candidate_spans[index] == span`` in every branch (including the
591	    all-furniture fallback), so a future caller consuming EITHER element is safe. Falsy
592	    candidates are SKIPPED but their positions are preserved. (The prior impl returned
593	    ``(0, <first non-falsy span>)`` in the all-furniture branch, where index 0 could point
594	    at a SKIPPED falsy span rather than the returned span — the off-by-one this fix closes.)
595	
596	    LIVE-CALLER (index-only) NOTE: ``live_retriever._build_provenance_quote`` uses only the
597	    index and reorders iff it is ``> 0``; there ``candidate_spans[0]`` is the ``head`` chunk,
598	    which is never falsy, so the first-non-falsy index is 0 in the all-furniture case => no
599	    reorder (the disclose/down-weight path stays owner) — unchanged by this fix."""
600	    spans = candidate_spans or []
601	    fallback_index = 0
602	    fallback_span = ""
603	    for i, span in enumerate(spans):
604	        if not span:
605	            continue  # skip falsy candidate but KEEP the original index position
606	        if not fallback_span:
607	            fallback_index = i  # REAL index of the first non-falsy span (all-furniture fallback)
608	            fallback_span = span
609	        if not _is_furniture_segment(span):
610	            return (i, span)
611	    return (fallback_index, fallback_span)
612	
613	
614	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
615	# I-deepfix-004 D (#1375) — CITED-WORK SPAN SCREEN (issue front-matter / masthead).
616	#
617	# WHY (distinct from the furniture screen above): the furniture screen catches a
618	# body that is MOSTLY masthead/nav/DOI chrome welded together. It does NOT catch a
619	# stored SPAN that is a journal ISSUE'S COVER / TABLE-OF-CONTENTS / MASTHEAD sitting
620	# at the head of an otherwise real multi-article container PDF. That is the
621	# I-deepfix-004 failure: a doi.org URL redirected to a whole issue PDF, the first
622	# ~2000 chars (= the cover/TOC/masthead) became the cited direct_quote, and a
623	# qualitative claim verified against it on 2 shared content words (the "wrong
624	# CITED-WORK, right topic" hole — every prior gate asked "is this junk?" / "is this
625	# on-topic?"; none asked "is this the cited ARTICLE?").
626	#
627	# ``is_issue_front_matter`` is a STRUCTURAL detector on the STORED SPAN (not the raw
628	# full body — a 40-page issue body is mostly article prose, so the density signal
629	# only reads true on the head SPAN that was actually stored). PRECISION-FIRST +
630	# FAIL-OPEN (any uncertainty ⇒ KEEP): calibrated on the real banked drb_78 corpus so
631	# genuine article heads / reference lists / an incidental "contents" mention never
632	# fire. NEVER used to DROP — only to mark a span ``wrong_content_span`` so the caller
633	# RE-EXTRACTS (step B) to RECOVER; if unrecovered the SOURCE is KEPT as a DISCLOSED
634	# DEGRADED row (§-1.3: recover→degrade→disclose, never delete a credible on-topic
635	# source; only the wrong SPAN is unusable for grounding).
636	#
637	# Both behaviours sit behind the default-ON ``PG_SPAN_CITED_WORK_SCREEN``; OFF => the
638	# functions are never called on the hot path => byte-identical.
639	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
640	
641	_ENV_SPAN_CITED_WORK_SCREEN = "PG_SPAN_CITED_WORK_SCREEN"
642	
643	# Minimum dot-leader / page-number TOC lines that must appear in the span for the
644	# structural TOC signal to fire. Calibrated on the real banked drb_78 corpus: genuine
645	# issue/report TOC heads carry 13–14 dot-leaders in the first 2000 chars, while every
646	# real article head + reference list + an incidental "contents" mention carries 0. A
647	# high floor (default 8) is precision-first — it never fires on a real article head.
648	_ENV_FRONT_MATTER_TOC_LINE_MIN = "PG_FRONT_MATTER_TOC_LINE_MIN"
649	_DEFAULT_FRONT_MATTER_TOC_LINE_MIN = 8
650	
651	# The structural verdict is meaningless on a tiny stub (the short-body shell gates own
652	# those); the screen only reads a body at/above this length (a masthead/TOC head span).
653	_ENV_FRONT_MATTER_MIN_BODY_CHARS = "PG_FRONT_MATTER_MIN_BODY_CHARS"
654	_DEFAULT_FRONT_MATTER_MIN_BODY_CHARS = 400
655	
656	# I-deepfix-004 F3: the screen reads ONLY the HEAD of the span, never the whole body.
657	# A legit LONG report / guideline / systematic review carries a normal contents index
658	# or list-of-tables DEEP in its body whose dot-leader lines would, SUMMED over the full
659	# body, cross the absolute TOC floor and FALSELY flag a real article. Capping the scan to
660	# the head keeps the calibration head-span-only regardless of the caller's span length
661	# (genuine issue/report TOC heads carry 13–14 dot-leaders in the first 2000 chars; every
662	# real article head carries 0). A head of <=0 disables the cap (whole-body scan).
663	_ENV_FRONT_MATTER_HEAD_CHARS = "PG_FRONT_MATTER_HEAD_CHARS"
664	_DEFAULT_FRONT_MATTER_HEAD_CHARS = 2000
665	
666	# Dot-leader run (three-or-more dots) followed by a page number (arabic or roman) — the
667	# canonical Table-of-Contents line shape, robust to the space-collapsed single-line PDF
668	# extraction output. High-precision: a run of ``...`` before a page number never occurs
669	# in real article prose.
670	_FRONT_MATTER_DOT_LEADER_RE = re.compile(r"\.{3,}\s*[ivxlcdm\d]{1,5}\b", re.IGNORECASE)
671	
672	# Contents / masthead vocabulary — REQUIRED as a co-signal for the ISSN masthead path
673	# (never fires alone; an incidental "contents" mention in a real article must not trip
674	# the screen — verified on the real corpus: netce "course/content" + a caregiving
675	# report both carry "contents" yet are genuine articles). Includes the Cyrillic
676	# journal-issue "СОДЕРЖАНИЕ" (contents) + "РЕДАКЦИОННАЯ КОЛЛЕГИЯ" (editorial board) for
677	# the dgpu-class Russian-journal masthead.
678	_FRONT_MATTER_CONTENTS_VOCAB: tuple[str, ...] = (
679	    "table of contents",
680	    "содержание",
681	    "editorial board",
682	    "editor-in-chief",
683	    "editorial office",
684	    "редакционная коллегия",
685	    "редакционный совет",
686	)
687	
688	# ISSN masthead marker (print/electronic serial number) — a strong journal-issue
689	# front-matter signal, but REQUIRED to co-occur with contents vocab so a reference
690	# list that merely cites an ISSN never trips the screen.
691	_FRONT_MATTER_ISSN_RE = re.compile(r"issn\s*:?\s*\d{4}-\d{3}[\dxX]", re.IGNORECASE)
692	
693	
694	def span_cited_work_screen_enabled() -> bool:
695	    """True (default) ⇒ the cited-work span screen (``is_issue_front_matter`` +
696	    ``identical_span_collision``) participates in the degraded-detection unions.
697	
698	    ``PG_SPAN_CITED_WORK_SCREEN=0`` (or off/false/no/disabled) ⇒ the screen is never
699	    consulted ⇒ byte-identical. Read at call time so tests toggle without re-import."""
700	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_SPAN_CITED_WORK_SCREEN, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
701	
702	
703	def _front_matter_toc_line_min() -> int:
704	    """Minimum dot-leader TOC lines for the structural TOC signal to fire."""
705	    return _env_int(_ENV_FRONT_MATTER_TOC_LINE_MIN, _DEFAULT_FRONT_MATTER_TOC_LINE_MIN)
706	
707	
708	def _front_matter_min_body_chars() -> int:
709	    """Minimum span length at/above which the front-matter screen reads a verdict."""
710	    return _env_int(_ENV_FRONT_MATTER_MIN_BODY_CHARS, _DEFAULT_FRONT_MATTER_MIN_BODY_CHARS)
711	
712	
713	def _front_matter_head_chars() -> int:
714	    """Character window at the HEAD of the span the screen scans (<=0 => whole body)."""
715	    return _env_int(_ENV_FRONT_MATTER_HEAD_CHARS, _DEFAULT_FRONT_MATTER_HEAD_CHARS)
716	
717	
718	def is_issue_front_matter(body: str) -> bool:
719	    """I-deepfix-004 D — True iff ``body`` (a STORED cited SPAN) is a journal-issue
720	    COVER / TABLE-OF-CONTENTS / MASTHEAD rather than the cited article's prose.
721	
722	    STRUCTURAL + DETERMINISTIC + PRECISION-FIRST + FAIL-OPEN (any uncertainty ⇒ KEEP,
723	    i.e. return ``False``). Two independent high-precision signals — either fires:
724	
725	      1. TOC-shaped: at least ``PG_FRONT_MATTER_TOC_LINE_MIN`` (default 8) dot-leader
726	         runs (``...`` before a page number). A real article head / reference list
727	         carries ZERO (calibrated on the real banked drb_78 corpus: genuine TOC heads
728	         carry 13–14, every real article head carries 0).
729	      2. ISSN masthead: an ``ISSN dddd-dddd`` marker AND contents/editorial-board vocab
730	         co-occur (both required — an incidental "contents" mention or a lone ISSN
731	         citation in real prose trips neither). Covers the Cyrillic dgpu-class masthead
732	         (``СОДЕРЖАНИЕ`` / ``РЕДАКЦИОННАЯ КОЛЛЕГИЯ`` + ISSN).
733	
734	    NEVER a DROP: a ``True`` verdict marks the SPAN ``wrong_content_span`` so the caller
735	    re-extracts (step B) to recover the real article, else keeps the SOURCE as a
736	    DISCLOSED DEGRADED row (§-1.3). Pure; no LLM / network / row mutation.
737	    """
738	    if not body:
739	        return False
740	    if len(body.strip()) < _front_matter_min_body_chars():
741	        return False  # fail-open: too short to read a structural verdict
742	    # I-deepfix-004 F3: read ONLY the HEAD of the span (a masthead/TOC lives at the
743	    # top). Scanning the FULL body let dot-leader lines from a normal contents index
744	    # / list-of-tables DEEP in a long report accumulate past the absolute TOC floor
745	    # and FALSELY flag a real article. Head-span-only regardless of caller span length.
746	    _head_chars = _front_matter_head_chars()
747	    head = body[:_head_chars] if _head_chars > 0 else body
748	    low = head.lower()
749	    # Signal 1 — dot-leader TOC density (any-length; the run-of-dots-before-a-page-number
750	    # shape never occurs in real article prose).
751	    if len(_FRONT_MATTER_DOT_LEADER_RE.findall(head)) >= _front_matter_toc_line_min():
752	        return True
753	    # Signal 2 — ISSN masthead AND contents/editorial vocab co-occur (both required).
754	    if _FRONT_MATTER_ISSN_RE.search(head) and any(
755	        marker in low for marker in _FRONT_MATTER_CONTENTS_VOCAB
756	    ):
757	        return True
758	    return False
759	
760	
761	def _collision_span_key(row: "dict") -> str:
762	    """The content-identity key for the identical-span collision screen: the STORED
763	    SPAN (``direct_quote`` / sliced text).
764	
765	    I-deepfix-004 F4 — NOT the whole-PDF ``fetched_blob_sha``: two CORRECTLY-sliced
766	    DISTINCT articles from one issue PDF share the same whole-PDF blob sha but carry
767	    DIFFERENT stored spans, so keying on the blob sha FALSELY merged them into a
768	    container collision. The span the pool actually STORES + CITES is the identity
769	    that matters — when slicing fails, the identical masthead TEXT still collides
770	    across DOIs via identical ``direct_quote`` (the anti-laundering intent holds);
771	    when slicing succeeds, the distinct sliced text correctly does not collide. The
772	    ``fetched_blob_sha`` stamp remains available for STEP-E blob consolidation
773	    elsewhere; it is simply not the span-collision key. Empty when no stored span is
774	    present (an empty-keyed row can never collide). Pure."""
775	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
776	        return ""
777	    quote = str(row.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()
778	    return f"q:{quote}" if quote else ""
779	
780	
781	def _collision_work_id(row: "dict") -> str:
782	    """The cited-WORK identity for the collision screen: the DOI (normalized) when
783	    present, else the source URL. Two rows with the SAME work-id sharing an identical
784	    span are legitimate duplicates (CONSOLIDATE, step E) — NOT a container collision.
785	    Two rows with DIFFERENT work-ids sharing an identical span ARE a multi-work
786	    container (one blob cited under many DOIs = the 31x-dgpu false corroboration)."""
787	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
788	        return ""
789	    doi = str(row.get("doi") or "").strip().lower()
790	    if doi:
791	        return f"doi:{doi}"
792	    url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "").strip().lower()
793	    return f"url:{url}"
794	
795	
796	def identical_span_collision(rows: "list[dict]") -> "set[str]":
797	    """I-deepfix-004 D — pool-level content-identity collision detector.
798	
799	    Returns the set of ``evidence_id`` values whose stored span is shared, VERBATIM, by
800	    at least two rows citing DIFFERENT cited works (different DOI/URL) — the signature of
801	    a single multi-article CONTAINER blob (e.g. a whole journal issue) laundered into N
802	    distinct citations. That is the 31x-dgpu false-corroboration class: 31 DOIs, one
803	    identical masthead blob.
804	
805	    NOT flagged: two rows with an identical span AND the SAME cited work (a legitimate
806	    duplicate → CONSOLIDATE in step E, never a container). A unique span → never flagged.
807	
808	    Pure; DETECTS only. The caller stamps ``wrong_content_span`` on the flagged rows so
809	    they route through re-extraction / disclosure — NEVER deletes a source (§-1.3)."""
810	    from collections import defaultdict
811	
812	    by_span: "dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]]" = defaultdict(list)
813	    for row in rows or []:
814	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
815	            continue
816	        span_key = _collision_span_key(row)
817	        if not span_key:
818	            continue
819	        eid = str(row.get("evidence_id") or "")
820	        work_id = _collision_work_id(row)
821	        by_span[span_key].append((eid, work_id))
822	
823	    flagged: "set[str]" = set()
824	    for members in by_span.values():
825	        if len(members) < 2:
826	            continue  # a unique span cannot be a container collision
827	        distinct_works = {work_id for _eid, work_id in members if work_id}
828	        if len(distinct_works) >= 2:
829	            # ≥2 DIFFERENT cited works share ONE identical span ⇒ multi-work container.
830	            for eid, _work_id in members:
831	                if eid:
832	                    flagged.add(eid)
833	    return flagged
834

1	"""Synthesis Prompt Constants (Fix R5-#5 + Citation Rules).
2	
3	Collects all prompt injection blocks that the v2 section writer system
4	prompt must include. Separating these from the synthesizer code makes
5	them testable, reviewable, and versionable.
6	
7	Usage:
8	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.synthesis_prompts import (
9	        PHANTOM_FIGURE_BAN,
10	        EVIDENCE_FIRST_RULES,
11	        ANALYTICAL_WRITING_RULES,
12	        SECTION_WRITER_SYSTEM_PROMPT,
13	    )
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import os
19	
20	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.citation_normalizer import CITATION_RULES  # v2 only
21	
22	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23	# FIX-CITE: v1/v3 citation rules using [CITE:evidence_id] format
24	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25	# The v2 pipeline uses [SRC-NNN] with a SourceRegistry that maps NNN->URL.
26	# The v1/v3 pipeline uses [CITE:evidence_id] throughout (section_writer.py,
27	# quality gate, citation density check, expansion, hallucination audit).
28	# Injecting CITATION_RULES ([SRC-NNN]) into the v1/v3 prompt causes the LLM
29	# to use a format that no downstream component can resolve, yielding 0 citations.
30	
31	CITE_EVIDENCE_RULES = """
32	CITATION FORMAT (MANDATORY — violations will fail quality gate):
33	- Cite sources inline as [CITE:evidence_id], using the exact evidence ID provided.
34	- For multiple sources: [CITE:ev_aaa][CITE:ev_bbb] (separate brackets).
35	- NEVER combine citations: [CITE:ev_aaa, ev_bbb] is FORBIDDEN.
36	- NEVER abbreviate or rename evidence IDs.
37	- NEVER use [SRC-NNN] format — use [CITE:evidence_id] ONLY.
38	- Every factual claim MUST have at least one [CITE:evidence_id] citation.
39	""".strip()
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Fix R5-#5: Phantom Figure Ban
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	# Evidence chunks from PDFs contain references to "Figure 4", "Table 2", etc.
45	# Since we killed Smart Art and don't render source images, these references
46	# produce phantom holes — the reader looks for Figure 4, finds nothing, and
47	# immediately recognizes the report as unverified AI output.
48	
49	PHANTOM_FIGURE_BAN = """
50	FIGURE/TABLE REFERENCE RULES (MANDATORY):
51	- Do NOT reference external figures, charts, tables, or appendices that you cannot display.
52	- Transform references into inline facts:
53	  BAD:  "As shown in Figure 2, the removal efficiency was 99.2%"
54	  GOOD: "The removal efficiency was 99.2%"
55	  BAD:  "Table 3 summarizes the cost comparison across all sites"
56	  GOOD: "Cost comparison across sites showed..."
57	  BAD:  "See Appendix B for the full dataset"
58	  GOOD: (omit entirely — the reader cannot see Appendix B)
59	- You MAY create your own markdown tables from data if the evidence supports it.
60	- You MUST NOT invent data to fill a table. Only tabulate facts present in the evidence.
61	""".strip()
62	
63	
64	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	# Evidence-First Writing Rules
66	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
67	
68	EVIDENCE_FIRST_RULES = """
69	EVIDENCE-FIRST WRITING RULES (MANDATORY):
70	- Every factual claim MUST be grounded in the provided evidence.
71	- Do NOT state facts from your training data — only from the evidence below.
72	- If the evidence is insufficient, say so honestly. Do NOT pad with filler.
73	- Filler phrases are BANNED: "It is important to note", "Furthermore",
74	  "In conclusion", "It should be noted that", "Interestingly".
75	- Write in active voice. Be direct. Be specific with numbers and units.
76	- If two sources disagree, present BOTH positions with citations.
77	""".strip()
78	
79	
80	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	# RC-2: Analytical Writing Rules (v3 hybrid prompt)
82	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
83	
84	ANALYTICAL_WRITING_RULES = """
85	ANALYTICAL WRITING RULES (MANDATORY):
86	- You are ANALYZING evidence, not restating it. Your job is to synthesize, compare, and evaluate.
87	- Every factual claim MUST be grounded in the provided evidence.
88	- Do NOT state facts from your training data — only from the evidence below.
89	
90	FIVE REQUIRED ANALYTICAL OPERATIONS:
91	1. AGGREGATE: When 3+ sources report similar findings, combine into one synthesized
92	   claim citing all sources. NEVER list source findings sequentially.
93	   BAD:  "Study A found 95%. Study B found 89%. Study C found 92%."
94	   GOOD: "Removal efficiencies ranged from 89-95% across three studies [1,2,3],
95	          with a median of 92%."
96	
97	2. COMPARE: When evidence differs by methodology, region, timeframe, or conditions,
98	   create explicit comparison paragraphs. Use "whereas", "in contrast", "compared to".
99	   BAD:  "Method A achieved X. Method B achieved Y."
100	   GOOD: "Method A achieved X under conditions P, whereas Method B achieved Y under
101	          conditions Q — the difference likely attributable to Z [1,2]."
102	
103	3. EXPLAIN — "SO WHAT" (HIGHEST PRIORITY): For EVERY quantitative finding you cite,
104	   you MUST immediately follow it with THREE layers of interpretation:
105	   a) CLINICAL MEANING: What does this number mean for a patient or clinician?
106	   b) CONFIDENCE: What limits this finding? (sample size, duration, heterogeneity)
107	   c) CONTRADICTION: Does any evidence challenge this? If so, explain the tension.
108	   Do NOT move to the next finding until you have addressed all three.
109	   BAD:  "HOMA-IR decreased by SMD -0.39 (95% CI: -0.65 to -0.12) [1]."
110	   GOOD: "HOMA-IR decreased by SMD -0.39 (95% CI: -0.65 to -0.12) [1] — a moderate
111	          improvement in insulin sensitivity that, if sustained, could reduce the
112	          10-year risk of type 2 diabetes progression. However, this estimate derives
113	          from studies with median 3-month follow-up, leaving the durability of this
114	          improvement uncharacterized. The confidence interval approaching zero suggests
115	          some populations may not benefit."
116	
117	4. TABULATE: When 3+ comparable data points exist (same measurement, different
118	   conditions/entities), you MUST present them as a markdown table with citations
119	   in each row. This is NOT optional.
120	
121	5. CHALLENGE: Each section MUST include at least one paragraph acknowledging
122	   limitations, contradictions, or gaps in the evidence base.
123	
124	MULTI-PERSPECTIVE CHECK (apply to each paragraph before finalizing):
125	Before writing each paragraph, consider it from THREE perspectives:
126	- CLINICIAN: "Would I change my practice based on this finding alone?"
127	- METHODOLOGIST: "What flaw in the study design could invalidate this?"
128	- PATIENT: "What does this actually mean for my daily health decisions?"
129	If any perspective raises a concern, address it in the text. This ensures
130	every paragraph contains interpretation, not just data reporting.
131	
132	HEDGING RULES:
133	- Limit "may", "could", "potentially" to MAX 4 per section.
134	- When evidence is strong (meta-analysis, large RCT), use DEFINITIVE language:
135	  BAD:  "Intermittent fasting may reduce body weight"
136	  GOOD: "Intermittent fasting reduces body weight (MD -4.30 kg) [CITE:ev_xxx]"
137	- Reserve hedging for genuinely uncertain findings (single study, small sample, animal model).
138	
139	BANNED PATTERNS:
140	- Sequential source summaries ("Study A found... Study B found... Study C found...")
141	- Data without interpretation ("X was Y" with no clinical meaning or confidence assessment)
142	- Filler phrases: "It is important to note", "Furthermore", "In conclusion",
143	  "It should be noted that", "Interestingly"
144	- Writing about evidence without citing it
145	- Padding beyond what evidence supports
146	""".strip()
147	
148	
149	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
150	# Anti-Hallucination Rules
151	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
152	
153	ANTI_HALLUCINATION_RULES = """
154	ANTI-HALLUCINATION RULES (MANDATORY):
155	- You may ONLY cite evidence IDs provided to you. Do NOT invent citation IDs.
156	- If you cannot find supporting evidence for a claim, DELETE the claim.
157	- Do NOT write "studies have shown" without a specific citation.
158	- Do NOT generalize beyond what the specific evidence states.
159	- Numerical values MUST match the evidence exactly — do not round or approximate.
160	""".strip()
161	
162	
163	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
164	# Composite system prompt for section writer
165	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
166	
167	def build_section_writer_prompt(
168	    n_evidence: int,
169	    suggested_words: int,
170	    analytical_focus: str = "",  # RC-3: from question decomposition
171	) -> str:
172	    """Build the complete section writer system prompt.
173	
174	    Combines all mandatory rules into a single, coherent system prompt.
175	    This is the ONE place where all prompt constraints live.
176	
177	    When PG_V3_ANALYTICAL_PROMPT=1, uses ANALYTICAL_WRITING_RULES
178	    instead of EVIDENCE_FIRST_RULES for deeper synthesis quality.
179	
180	    Args:
181	        n_evidence: Number of evidence pieces assigned to this section.
182	        suggested_words: Dynamic word target (from SectionSpec.effective_target_words).
183	        analytical_focus: Optional analytical focus question from RC-3
184	            question decomposition. Prepended to the prompt when provided.
185	
186	    Returns:
187	        Complete system prompt string.
188	    """
189	    use_analytical = os.getenv("PG_V3_ANALYTICAL_PROMPT", "0") == "1"
190	    writing_rules = ANALYTICAL_WRITING_RULES if use_analytical else EVIDENCE_FIRST_RULES
191	
192	    focus_block = ""
193	    if analytical_focus:
194	        focus_block = f"\nANALYTICAL FOCUS: {analytical_focus}\n"
195	
196	    return f"""You are writing one section of a research report. You have {n_evidence} evidence pieces.
197	{focus_block}
198	{writing_rules}
199	
200	{CITE_EVIDENCE_RULES}
201	
202	{PHANTOM_FIGURE_BAN}
203	
204	{ANTI_HALLUCINATION_RULES}
205	
206	WORD TARGET:
207	- Target approximately {suggested_words} words based on available evidence.
208	- MAXIMUM 1500 words per section. Do NOT exceed this limit.
209	- {n_evidence} evidence pieces support roughly {suggested_words} words of analysis.
210	- Do NOT pad beyond what the evidence supports. Quality over quantity.
211	- If evidence is thin, write a shorter, honest section rather than padding with filler.
212	
213	GRADE EVIDENCE CERTAINTY:
214	- When evidence has a [GRADE: high/moderate/low/very_low] tag, INCLUDE the rating
215	  in your prose. Example: "...with moderate certainty (GRADE) [CITE:ev_xxx]"
216	- This helps readers assess confidence in each finding.
217	
218	KEY FINDINGS:
219	- End each section with a "**Key Findings**" subsection (3-5 bullet points).
220	- Each bullet must cite at least one source.
221	- Bullets must be specific (include numbers, units, conditions) not vague summaries."""
222

1	"""I-deepfix-001 B14 (2026-06-28) — title<->body cross-wiring consistency gate.
2	
3	A mis-stitched source (the serper/openalex METADATA title belongs to a different
4	page than the FETCHED body — a redirect-to-homepage, a wrong-anchor crawl, a
5	chrome shell whose title is the site name) makes the pipeline reason about ONE
6	source under TWO identities. This is CiteCheck's title-fidelity acceptance signal
7	applied to metadata-title-vs-fetched-body.
8	
9	POSTURE (§-1.3 weight-not-filter): this NEVER drops a source. On a confirmed
10	mismatch it (a) RE-DERIVES the title from the body and (b) sets
11	``identity_consistent=False`` carried onto the evidence row, so downstream
12	generation / dedup can avoid reasoning about a corrupt identity. The source is
13	QUARANTINED to "title corrected + flagged", not deleted. Faithfulness engine
14	(strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance / span-grounding) UNTOUCHED.
15	
16	TWO-STAGE (CiteCheck/CiteAudit pattern):
17	  1. cheap normalized edit-distance / token-overlap PRE-SCREEN on every source;
18	  2. the LOCKED-SLATE reranker/embed similarity (reused via an injected callable —
19	     the same Qwen3 path content_relevance_judge uses) ONLY on flagged sources.
20	
21	Pure leaf module: no network, no model import. The slate similarity is provided
22	by an injected ``similarity_fn(a, b) -> float`` so this is unit-testable offline
23	and the caller wires the real Qwen3 reranker/embedder. All knobs are env (LAW VI).
24	"""
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import re
30	from dataclasses import dataclass
31	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
32	
33	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.title_body_consistency")
34	
35	_ENV_FLAG = "PG_TITLE_BODY_CONSISTENCY"
36	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
37	
38	# Minimum slate-similarity below which the metadata title and the body are judged
39	# mis-stitched (env-overridable, LAW VI). Default 0.35 per the B14 fix plan.
40	_ENV_MIN_SIM = "PG_TITLE_BODY_MIN_SIM"
41	_DEFAULT_MIN_SIM = 0.35
42	
43	# Edit-distance / token-overlap PRE-SCREEN: a source whose metadata title and
44	# body-derived title already overlap well skips the (costly) slate similarity.
45	_ENV_PRESCREEN_OVERLAP = "PG_TITLE_BODY_PRESCREEN_OVERLAP"
46	_DEFAULT_PRESCREEN_OVERLAP = 0.5
47	
48	# Head window of the body fed to the slate similarity (topicality, bounded cost).
49	_ENV_BODY_CHARS = "PG_TITLE_BODY_BODY_CHARS"
50	_DEFAULT_BODY_CHARS = 512
51	
52	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9\-]+")
53	
54	
55	def title_body_consistency_enabled() -> bool:
56	    """B14 kill-switch. DEFAULT OFF (the fix plan's stated default) so the OFF path
57	    is byte-identical and the new evidence-row keys are ABSENT. Set
58	    ``PG_TITLE_BODY_CONSISTENCY=1`` to activate the gate."""
59	    return os.getenv(_ENV_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
60	
61	
62	def _min_sim() -> float:
63	    return _env_float(_ENV_MIN_SIM, _DEFAULT_MIN_SIM)
64	
65	
66	def _prescreen_overlap() -> float:
67	    return _env_float(_ENV_PRESCREEN_OVERLAP, _DEFAULT_PRESCREEN_OVERLAP)
68	
69	
70	def _body_chars() -> int:
71	    try:
72	        return max(64, int(os.getenv(_ENV_BODY_CHARS, str(_DEFAULT_BODY_CHARS))))
73	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
74	        return _DEFAULT_BODY_CHARS
75	
76	
77	def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
78	    raw = os.getenv(name, "").strip()
79	    if not raw:
80	        return default
81	    try:
82	        val = float(raw)
83	    except ValueError:
84	        return default
85	    # Clamp to [0, 1] — a similarity threshold outside that range is a misconfig.
86	    return min(max(val, 0.0), 1.0)
87	
88	
89	def _tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
90	    if not text:
91	        return set()
92	    return {t.lower() for t in _TOKEN_RE.findall(text) if len(t) > 2}
93	
94	
95	def _token_overlap(a: str, b: str) -> float:
96	    """Containment overlap |a∩b| / min(|a|,|b|) on token sets — the cheap prescreen
97	    (a short real title fully inside the body title scores ~1.0)."""
98	    ta, tb = _tokens(a), _tokens(b)
99	    if not ta or not tb:
100	        return 0.0
101	    inter = ta & tb
102	    smaller = min(len(ta), len(tb))
103	    return len(inter) / smaller if smaller else 0.0
104	
105	
106	@dataclass
107	class TitleBodyVerdict:
108	    """Per-source identity outcome (a flag + a corrected title, never a drop)."""
109	
110	    identity_consistent: bool
111	    title_source: str          # 'metadata' (kept) or 'rederived_from_body'
112	    resolved_title: str
113	    metadata_title: str
114	    body_title: str
115	    prescreen_overlap: float
116	    slate_similarity: Optional[float]  # None when the prescreen already passed
117	    reason: str = ""
118	
119	
120	def check_title_body_consistency(
121	    metadata_title: str,
122	    body_title: str,
123	    body_text: str,
124	    *,
125	    similarity_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], float]] = None,
126	) -> TitleBodyVerdict:
127	    """Judge whether ``metadata_title`` belongs to the fetched body.
128	
129	    Stage 1 — cheap token-overlap prescreen. If the metadata title and the body-
130	    derived title overlap >= the prescreen floor, accept (no slate call).
131	    Stage 2 — only when the prescreen flags, call the injected ``similarity_fn``
132	    (the locked Qwen3 reranker/embed) between the metadata title and
133	    (body_title + body head). Below ``PG_TITLE_BODY_MIN_SIM`` ⇒ mis-stitched:
134	    RE-DERIVE the title from the body and flag ``identity_consistent=False``.
135	
136	    NEVER drops a source — the worst outcome is a corrected title + a flag.
137	    """
138	    meta = (metadata_title or "").strip()
139	    body_t = (body_title or "").strip()
140	    # No body title to compare against — cannot judge; keep metadata, consistent.
141	    if not body_t:
142	        return TitleBodyVerdict(
143	            identity_consistent=True, title_source="metadata",
144	            resolved_title=meta, metadata_title=meta, body_title=body_t,
145	            prescreen_overlap=1.0, slate_similarity=None,
146	            reason="no body title — cannot judge, kept",
147	        )
148	    overlap = _token_overlap(meta, body_t)
149	    if overlap >= _prescreen_overlap():
150	        return TitleBodyVerdict(
151	            identity_consistent=True, title_source="metadata",
152	            resolved_title=meta, metadata_title=meta, body_title=body_t,
153	            prescreen_overlap=overlap, slate_similarity=None,
154	            reason="prescreen overlap passed",
155	        )
156	    # Flagged by the cheap prescreen — escalate to the slate similarity.
157	    sim: Optional[float] = None
158	    scorer_errored = False
159	    if similarity_fn is not None:
160	        body_window = (body_text or "")[: _body_chars()]
161	        try:
162	            sim = float(similarity_fn(meta, (body_t + " " + body_window).strip()))
163	        except Exception as exc:  # a slate failure must never drop/flag a source
164	            logger.warning(
165	                "[title_body] slate similarity failed (%s) — keeping metadata "
166	                "title (no drop, no flag on a scorer error).", str(exc)[:160],
167	            )
168	            sim = None
169	            scorer_errored = True
170	    # A scorer ERROR must NEVER flip the identity (no flag on a bug — LAW II loud
171	    # degrade keeps the metadata title). Only an actual slate signal below the floor
172	    # re-derives; with no slate fn at all we fall back to the prescreen overlap so
173	    # an essentially-zero-overlap title with no slate is still caught.
174	    if scorer_errored:
175	        return TitleBodyVerdict(
176	            identity_consistent=True, title_source="metadata",
177	            resolved_title=meta, metadata_title=meta, body_title=body_t,
178	            prescreen_overlap=overlap, slate_similarity=None,
179	            reason="slate scorer errored — kept metadata (no flag on a bug)",
180	        )
181	    effective = sim if sim is not None else overlap
182	    if effective < _min_sim():
183	        return TitleBodyVerdict(
184	            identity_consistent=False, title_source="rederived_from_body",
185	            resolved_title=body_t, metadata_title=meta, body_title=body_t,
186	            prescreen_overlap=overlap, slate_similarity=sim,
187	            reason="title<->body mismatch below floor — title re-derived, flagged",
188	        )
189	    return TitleBodyVerdict(
190	        identity_consistent=True, title_source="metadata",
191	        resolved_title=meta, metadata_title=meta, body_title=body_t,
192	        prescreen_overlap=overlap, slate_similarity=sim,
193	        reason="slate similarity cleared the floor",
194	    )
195	
196	
197	def consistency_keys(verdict: TitleBodyVerdict) -> dict[str, Any]:
198	    """The evidence-row keys to merge ON the B14 ON path. ABSENT on the OFF path
199	    so the OFF evidence row is byte-identical."""
200	    return {
201	        "identity_consistent": verdict.identity_consistent,
202	        "title_source": verdict.title_source,
203	    }
204

content.text: Both clean. Continuing with topic_relevance_gate.py, ttddr_loop.py, verify_context.py, verify_schemas.py.

content.content:
1	"""WS-15 — TTD-DR coverage loop (Test-Time Diffusion Deep Research). PURE decision logic.
2	
3	TTD-DR (arXiv:2507.16075, Han et al., Google, 2025 — "Deep Researcher with
4	Test-Time Diffusion") conceptualises report writing as an iterative *diffusion*:
5	start from a preliminary DRAFT (an updatable skeleton), then progressively
6	"denoise" it by cycles of gap-finding -> targeted retrieval -> revision. It is
7	the mechanism the current DRB-II SOTA uses to lift COVERAGE / recall.
8	
9	Paper Algorithm 1 (Denoising with Retrieval), verbatim shape:
10	
11	    R_0 = initial draft
12	    for t in 1..N:
13	        Q_t = M_Q(q, R_{t-1})        # next question / coverage gap from the draft
14	        A_t = M_A(Q_t)               # retrieve an answer for that gap
15	        R_t = M_R(q, R_{t-1}, Q, A)  # revise (denoise) the draft with A_t
16	        if exit_loop: break
17	
18	This module implements that loop with the four model roles INJECTED as callables
19	(`draft_fn` = the skeleton generator, `gap_fn` = M_Q made explicit as coverage-gap
20	detection, `retrieve_fn` = M_A, `revise_fn` = M_R). Nothing here constructs an HTTP
21	client, loads a model, or bills a token: the caller wires the real roles; the tests
22	wire capture-only fakes. So it is fully OFFLINE-testable and touches no network at
23	import or test time.
24	
25	BOUNDS (the paper leaves `exit_loop` implementation-defined; the WS-15 plan
26	correction makes it a HARD triad so the loop can never run away):
27	  * `max_rounds`  — bounds the revision-round count (the paper's fixed N). This is
28	    the GUARANTEED termination backstop — always enforced.
29	  * `wall_seconds`— wall-clock deadline (monotonic; the clock is injectable so wall
30	    stops are deterministic offline). `None` disables only this early exit.
31	  * `cost_budget` — spend gate; the cumulative-cost probe is injected (`cost_fn`),
32	    so the caller wires the real cost ledger. `None` disables only this early exit.
33	The loop STOPS on the first of: gap_fn returns no new gaps (converged — the ideal
34	exit) OR any bound trips.
35	
36	§-1.3 (WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE, never FILTER-and-DROP): this module ADDS retrieved
37	evidence and REVISES the draft to widen coverage. It NEVER verifies, drops, caps,
38	or thins anything. The FROZEN faithfulness engine (strict_verify / provenance /
39	NLI / 4-role / span-grounding) still gates every revised claim DOWNSTREAM,
40	unchanged — this orchestrator is upstream of it and touches none of it.
41	
42	`PG_TTDDR_ENABLED` is DEFAULT-OFF: TTD-DR is a NEW opt-in capability wired into no
43	existing path, so the whole module stays dormant (never invokes a callable) until
44	the coverage run explicitly turns it on. When off, `ttddr_refine` short-circuits
45	and returns the initial draft untouched.
46	"""
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	import logging
50	import os
51	import time
52	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional, Sequence
53	
54	from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.saturation import marginal_novelty
55	
56	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.ttddr_loop")
57	
58	# ── stop reasons (module-level constants; allowed per CLAUDE.md §4.1) ──────────
59	STOP_NO_GAPS = "no_gaps"          # gap_fn found nothing more to close — converged
60	STOP_MAX_ROUNDS = "max_rounds"    # revision-round count bound hit
61	STOP_WALL = "wall"                # wall-clock deadline hit
62	STOP_COST = "cost"                # cumulative-cost budget hit
63	STOP_SATURATION = "saturation"    # source-yield saturated (marginal novelty < eps)
64	STOP_DISABLED = "disabled"        # PG_TTDDR_ENABLED off — loop never ran
65	
66	# ── default-OFF flag idiom (NEW opt-in capability; ON only on an explicit token) ──
67	_ENV_TTDDR_ENABLED = "PG_TTDDR_ENABLED"
68	_ENABLE_ON_VALUES = ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
69	
70	# I4 — source-yield saturation bound. `PG_TTDDR_SATURATION_EPS` is the marginal-
71	# novelty floor: once a revision round's TARGETED retrieval yields a NEW-source
72	# fraction below this floor, firing further rounds only re-fetches sources the
73	# draft already carries, so the loop stops SPENDING on rounds that add no new
74	# evidence. This is a COMPUTE bound keyed to source yield — it never drops, caps,
75	# or thins any retrieved source (every retrieved row this round is still folded
76	# into the draft and still gates through the frozen faithfulness engine
77	# downstream). It bounds how many MORE rounds run, not how much evidence renders.
78	_ENV_TTDDR_SATURATION_EPS = "PG_TTDDR_SATURATION_EPS"
79	_DEFAULT_SATURATION_EPS = 0.05
80	
81	
82	def ttddr_enabled() -> bool:
83	    """`PG_TTDDR_ENABLED` — DEFAULT-OFF. ON only on an explicit on token
84	    ('1'/'true'/'yes'/'on'); unset / empty / anything else -> OFF.
85	
86	    Default-OFF is correct here: TTD-DR is a brand-new capability wired into no
87	    existing path, so it must be opt-in for the coverage run and dormant elsewhere.
88	    """
89	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_TTDDR_ENABLED, "").strip().lower() in _ENABLE_ON_VALUES
90	
91	
92	def ttddr_saturation_eps() -> float:
93	    """`PG_TTDDR_SATURATION_EPS` — the marginal-novelty floor for the source-yield
94	    saturation bound (I4). Unset / invalid -> `_DEFAULT_SATURATION_EPS` (0.05).
95	
96	    The value is a FRACTION in [0, 1]: a round whose targeted retrieval brings a
97	    novel-source fraction below this floor is treated as saturated. A caller wires
98	    this (with a `rows_of` extractor) so the default TTD-DR run is bounded by
99	    source yield in addition to rounds/wall/cost. It is a spend bound, never a
100	    breadth cap — see the module-level note.
101	    """
102	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_TTDDR_SATURATION_EPS, "").strip()
103	    if not raw:
104	        return _DEFAULT_SATURATION_EPS
105	    try:
106	        val = float(raw)
107	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
108	        return _DEFAULT_SATURATION_EPS
109	    # Clamp to a sane fraction; a nonsensical value must not disable the bound.
110	    if val < 0.0:
111	        return 0.0
112	    if val > 1.0:
113	        return 1.0
114	    return val
115	
116	
117	def _as_gap_list(gaps: Any) -> list[Any]:
118	    """Materialise gap_fn's output to a list; None / falsy -> [] (converged)."""
119	    if not gaps:
120	        return []
121	    return list(gaps)
122	
123	
124	def ttddr_refine(
125	    question: str,
126	    initial_draft: Optional[Any] = None,
127	    *,
128	    draft_fn: Optional[Callable[[str], Any]] = None,
129	    gap_fn: Callable[[str, Any], Sequence[Any]],
130	    retrieve_fn: Callable[[str, Sequence[Any]], Any],
131	    revise_fn: Callable[[str, Any, Sequence[Any], Any], Any],
132	    max_rounds: int,
133	    # WS-15 P1 fix (Codex waveDE gate): all THREE bounds must be active by DEFAULT. max_rounds is required;
134	    # wall_seconds + cost_budget now default to real finite ceilings (NOT None), so the default loop is
135	    # fully bounded on rounds AND wall-clock AND cost. Passing None still explicitly opts a single bound
136	    # out for a caller that wires its own guard, but a default invocation can never run unbounded.
137	    wall_seconds: Optional[float] = 1800.0,
138	    cost_budget: Optional[float] = 50.0,
139	    # I4 — source-yield saturation bound (a COMPUTE bound, never a breadth cap).
140	    # `saturation_eps` is the marginal-novelty floor; `rows_of` maps a round's
141	    # retrieved evidence to its source rows (each row carrying `source_url`, the
142	    # live-retriever field). BOTH must be wired for the bound to act — the loop
143	    # cannot measure source yield without knowing how to read rows out of the
144	    # injected retrieve_fn's return. When either is None the bound is inert and
145	    # the loop keeps its rounds/wall/cost/no-gap behaviour unchanged.
146	    saturation_eps: Optional[float] = None,
147	    rows_of: Optional[Callable[[Any], Sequence[Any]]] = None,
148	    clock: Callable[[], float] = time.monotonic,
149	    cost_fn: Callable[[], float] = lambda: 0.0,
150	    log: Optional[Callable[[str], None]] = None,
151	) -> dict:
152	    """Run the bounded TTD-DR denoising-with-retrieval loop (paper Algorithm 1).
153	
154	    Injected role contracts (the caller wires real models; tests wire fakes):
155	      * `draft_fn(question) -> draft`                       — the R_0 skeleton, used
156	        ONLY when `initial_draft` is None.
157	      * `gap_fn(question, draft) -> Sequence[gap]`          — M_Q as explicit
158	        coverage-gap detection on the CURRENT draft; empty return == converged.
159	      * `retrieve_fn(question, gaps) -> evidence`           — M_A, targeted retrieval
160	        for the detected gaps.
161	      * `revise_fn(question, draft, gaps, evidence) -> draft` — M_R, denoise/revise
162	        the draft with the retrieved evidence.
163	
164	    Bounds (checked at the top of every round, in this priority order):
165	        wall_seconds -> cost_budget -> max_rounds -> gap detection.
166	    `max_rounds` is the guaranteed termination backstop and is always enforced;
167	    `wall_seconds` / `cost_budget` are additional early exits (`None` disables the
168	    corresponding early exit only).
169	
170	    Returns:
171	        {
172	          "final_draft": Any,      # the last revised draft (R_final)
173	          "rounds": int,           # revision rounds actually executed
174	          "gaps_closed": int,      # cumulative count of gaps addressed by a revision
175	          "stop_reason": str,      # one of the STOP_* constants
176	          "elapsed_seconds": float,
177	          "cost_spent": float,
178	          "gap_history": list[int],# len(gaps) closed per executed round
179	        }
180	
181	    This module NEVER verifies: it only orchestrates draft/gap/retrieve/revise. The
182	    frozen faithfulness engine gates every revised claim downstream, unchanged.
183	    """
184	    _log = log or (lambda _m: None)
185	
186	    # DEFAULT-OFF gate — when off, invoke NO callable and return the draft untouched.
187	    if not ttddr_enabled():
188	        _log("[ttddr] PG_TTDDR_ENABLED off — loop skipped")
189	        return {
190	            "final_draft": initial_draft,
191	            "rounds": 0,
192	            "gaps_closed": 0,
193	            "stop_reason": STOP_DISABLED,
194	            "elapsed_seconds": 0.0,
195	            "cost_spent": 0.0,
196	            "gap_history": [],
197	        }
198	
199	    if initial_draft is None:
200	        if draft_fn is None:
201	            raise ValueError(
202	                "ttddr_refine requires either initial_draft or draft_fn to seed R_0"
203	            )
204	        initial_draft = draft_fn(question)
205	
206	    draft: Any = initial_draft
207	    rounds = 0
208	    gaps_closed = 0
209	    gap_history: list[int] = []
210	    # I4 — source-yield saturation state. `cumulative_rows` is the running set of
211	    # source rows seen across all prior rounds (the novelty baseline); it only
212	    # ACCUMULATES — no row is ever removed, capped, or thinned. `novelty_history`
213	    # records the per-round novel-source fraction for observability.
214	    saturation_active = saturation_eps is not None and rows_of is not None
215	    cumulative_rows: list[Any] = []
216	    novelty_history: list[float] = []
217	    t0 = clock()
218	    now = t0
219	    spent = cost_fn()
220	
221	    def _finish(reason: str) -> dict:
222	        return {
223	            "final_draft": draft,
224	            "rounds": rounds,
225	            "gaps_closed": gaps_closed,
226	            "stop_reason": reason,
227	            "elapsed_seconds": max(0.0, now - t0),
228	            "cost_spent": spent,
229	            "gap_history": gap_history,
230	            "novelty_history": novelty_history,
231	        }
232	
233	    while True:
234	        now = clock()
235	        # 1) wall-clock deadline (early exit; None disables).
236	        if wall_seconds is not None and (now - t0) >= wall_seconds:
237	            _log(f"[ttddr] wall deadline hit at round={rounds} "
238	                 f"(elapsed={now - t0:.3f}s >= {wall_seconds}s) -> STOP_WALL")
239	            return _finish(STOP_WALL)
240	        # 2) cost budget (early exit; None disables).
241	        spent = cost_fn()
242	        if cost_budget is not None and spent >= cost_budget:
243	            _log(f"[ttddr] cost budget hit at round={rounds} "
244	                 f"(spent={spent} >= {cost_budget}) -> STOP_COST")
245	            return _finish(STOP_COST)
246	        # 3) round-count backstop (always enforced -> guarantees termination).
247	        if rounds >= max_rounds:
248	            _log(f"[ttddr] max_rounds hit (rounds={rounds} >= {max_rounds}) "
249	                 "-> STOP_MAX_ROUNDS")
250	            return _finish(STOP_MAX_ROUNDS)
251	
252	        # 4) gap detection on the current draft (M_Q). Empty -> converged.
253	        gaps = _as_gap_list(gap_fn(question, draft))
254	        if not gaps:
255	            _log(f"[ttddr] no residual gaps at round={rounds} -> STOP_NO_GAPS")
256	            return _finish(STOP_NO_GAPS)
257	
258	        # Fire one denoising round: targeted retrieve (M_A) then revise (M_R).
259	        evidence = retrieve_fn(question, gaps)
260	
261	        # I4 — measure this round's source yield BEFORE folding its rows into the
262	        # running baseline: novelty = fraction of THIS round's retrieved sources
263	        # not already seen in prior rounds. `marginal_novelty` reuses the shared
264	        # canonical-URL identity (saturation.py -> run_diff), so re-fetched
265	        # sources collapse and only genuinely-new sources count.
266	        round_novelty: Optional[float] = None
267	        if saturation_active:
268	            this_rows = list(rows_of(evidence) or [])
269	            round_novelty = marginal_novelty(cumulative_rows, this_rows)
270	            novelty_history.append(round_novelty)
271	            # ACCUMULATE only — never drop/cap. Every retrieved row stays in the
272	            # baseline and is still folded into the draft below.
273	            cumulative_rows.extend(this_rows)
274	
275	        # Always fold the retrieved evidence into the draft (consolidate, never
276	        # drop): even a saturating round's evidence widens the draft's coverage.
277	        draft = revise_fn(question, draft, gaps, evidence)
278	        rounds += 1
279	        gaps_closed += len(gaps)
280	        gap_history.append(len(gaps))
281	        _log(f"[ttddr] round={rounds} closed {len(gaps)} gap(s) "
282	             f"(cumulative gaps_closed={gaps_closed}"
283	             + (f", novelty={round_novelty:.3f}" if round_novelty is not None else "")
284	             + ")")
285	
286	        # I4 — source-yield saturation early exit. Requires a PRIOR round to
287	        # compare against (rounds >= 2), so the first retrieval never trips it.
288	        # Once a round's novel-source fraction falls below the floor, further
289	        # rounds only re-fetch known sources: STOP spending. This bounds compute,
290	        # not breadth — this round's evidence is already folded in above.
291	        if saturation_active and rounds >= 2 and round_novelty is not None \
292	                and round_novelty < saturation_eps:
293	            _log(f"[ttddr] source yield saturated at round={rounds} "
294	                 f"(novelty={round_novelty:.3f} < eps={saturation_eps}) "
295	                 "-> STOP_SATURATION")
296	            return _finish(STOP_SATURATION)
297

1	"""
2	Semantic topic-relevance gate — I-scope-001 (#1244).
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS (grounded diagnosis, do not re-derive):
5	A consolidated drb_72 breadth run cited 76 distinct sources with 0
6	fabrication, but ~9 were contamination — including 4 OFF-TOPIC-but-CREDIBLE
7	journals (spinal-cord stimulation, blockchain-sustainability, etc.). The
8	tier system rates CREDIBILITY, not RELEVANCE, so a credible-but-irrelevant
9	journal passes the tier gate. The lexical/embedding relevance floor ALSO
10	fails: en.wikipedia.org scored 0.583 relevance (above the clean median
11	0.500) because contaminants share generic content words with on-topic
12	sources. So neither tier nor embedding similarity can separate
13	off-topic-credible from on-topic — a TOPIC gate (semantic ON/OFF judgement
14	on the research-question domain) is required.
15	
16	This module is the pure, LLM-based topic gate. It is DEFAULT-OFF: the
17	orchestrator (run_honest_sweep_r3.py) only calls it when PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE
18	is truthy, and passes the production LLM callable + batch size. Keeping all
19	batching / parsing / exemption logic here (pure) means the gate is fully
20	unit-testable with a stub `llm_callable`, with NO OpenRouter key required.
21	
22	FAITHFULNESS LOCK: this gate is SELECTION-SIDE ONLY. It can only SUBTRACT a
23	candidate source from the pool handed to the generator; it NEVER edits a
24	sentence, span, or citation. strict_verify / the NLI entailment judge / the
25	4-role D8 audit / provenance are UNTOUCHED — every surviving sentence still
26	passes the identical faithfulness stack. Subtraction cannot fabricate.
27	
28	FAIL-OPEN CONTRACT (LAW II — never drop on uncertainty):
29	The gate drops a source ONLY on an explicit, confident OFF verdict. It KEEPS
30	the source (fail-open) on:
31	  - any LLM exception,
32	  - a returned verdict count that does not match the requested count,
33	  - any unparseable / unrecognised verdict line,
34	  - an empty / missing title+snippet (nothing to judge on).
35	A marquee / required-entity anchor is NEVER dropped, regardless of verdict.
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import logging
41	import os
42	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
43	from typing import Any, Callable
44	
45	_LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
46	
47	# Default batch size for PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_BATCH — how many sources are
48	# classified per LLM call. Bounds cost: one call covers up to this many
49	# sources. A small denominator keeps the prompt short + the parse simple.
50	_DEFAULT_TOPIC_BATCH = 25
51	
52	# Cap on the title+snippet length fed per source so a batch prompt stays
53	# bounded even with long live-retriever statements.
54	_MAX_SNIPPET_CHARS = 320

57	def topic_gate_enabled() -> bool:
58	    """Kill-switch ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE`` (default ON — I-deepfix-001 DEFER-1).
59	
60	    Default flipped ``"0"->"1"`` (2026-06-30): the gate is the SEMANTIC
61	    discriminator the cite-surface off-topic suppression keys on (a lexical floor
62	    cannot separate on-topic from off-topic — disproven on drb_72). It is §-1.3
63	    keystone-compatible: the DEFAULT verdict is DEMOTE-not-DROP (every source stays
64	    in the pool, the confident-OFF ones carry the ``topic_offtopic_demoted``
65	    sidecar), so turning it ON adds a disclosed WEIGHT, never a hard filter. Set
66	    ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE=0`` to restore the byte-identical legacy (gate never
67	    called) behaviour."""
68	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE", "1").strip().lower()
69	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")
70	
71	
72	def resume_run_topic_judge_enabled() -> bool:
73	    """Kill-switch ``PG_RESUME_RUN_TOPIC_JUDGE`` (default OFF — I-deepfix-001 wave-2).
74	
75	    A RESUME normally SKIPS this gate (the orchestrator guards the call with
76	    ``not _resume_active`` because the corpus_snapshot is reloaded post-selection). That
77	    leaves every reloaded row UNJUDGED, so an off-topic source can leak into the finding
78	    surface. When this flag is set, the orchestrator ALSO runs the EXISTING
79	    :func:`classify_topic_relevance` on a resume, so the reloaded rows get a topic verdict
80	    stamped. Default OFF => the ``not _resume_active`` short-circuit holds => byte-identical
81	    (the judge is not run on a resume, exactly as before)."""
82	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_RESUME_RUN_TOPIC_JUDGE", "").strip().lower()
83	    return raw in ("1", "true", "on", "yes", "enabled")
84

86	def mark_topic_judge_ran() -> None:
87	    """Set the run-scoped signal ``PG_TOPIC_JUDGE_RAN=1`` the moment the topic judge has
88	    executed this run (fresh or the resume path opened by
89	    :func:`resume_run_topic_judge_enabled`). The downstream unjudged-topic quarantine
90	    (``weighted_enrichment.topic_judge_ran`` / ``partition_unjudged_topic_rows``) reads this
91	    to prove the judge ran — so a row that STILL lacks a verdict is a genuine leak
92	    (quarantinable), NEVER a legitimately-skipped-judge false positive. Process/run-scoped
93	    (each sweep is its own process); the quarantine ALSO derives the same fact from the data,
94	    so this write is the explicit belt to that suspenders."""
95	    os.environ["PG_TOPIC_JUDGE_RAN"] = "1"

98	def topic_gate_hard_drop_enabled() -> bool:
99	    """LEGACY escape hatch ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP`` (default OFF).
100	
101	    §-1.3 (WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER — the operator names the "scope hard-filter" as a
102	    BANNED anti-pattern; the ONLY hard gate is the faithfulness engine): a
103	    confident-OFF source is, by DEFAULT, KEPT in the pool and DEMOTED (disclosed
104	    as off-topic), NEVER hard-dropped. Topicality is a WEIGHT, not a DROP — the
105	    source still flows to composition where the UNCHANGED strict_verify / NLI /
106	    4-role faithfulness engine is the only gate. Set this flag truthy ONLY to
107	    restore the pre-I-deepfix-001 hard-drop (audit / reversal); the default
108	    keep-all + demote is the §-1.3-correct behavior."""
109	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP", "0").strip().lower()
110	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")

113	def topic_gate_subject_aspect_split_enabled() -> bool:
114	    """Flag ``PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT`` (default ON — I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3).
115	
116	    Splits the single confident-OFF verdict into two kinds:
117	      - ``OFF_ASPECT`` — the SAME subject entity but a DIFFERENT aspect / use-case /
118	        population than the question asks about (a topic-adjacent hub, e.g. an
119	        education-AI paper for a labor-market question). DEMOTE-and-keep, NEVER deletable.
120	      - ``OFF_SUBJECT`` — a CLEARLY DIFFERENT subject entity (different field / domain —
121	        scholar-mill / unrelated-domain junk). This is the ONLY deletable OFF: it alone
122	        carries the ``topic_off_subject=True`` sidecar the downstream junk-deletion gate
123	        keys on.
124	    A legacy plain ``OFF`` parses as OFF_ASPECT (conservative — never delete on the old
125	    verdict form). Set ``PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT=0`` to restore the
126	    byte-identical legacy ON/OFF prompt + parser (no ``topic_off_subject`` stamp)."""
127	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT", "1").strip().lower()
128	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")

131	def junk_chrome_before_offtopic_enabled() -> bool:
132	    """Flag ``PG_JUNK_CHROME_BEFORE_OFFTOPIC`` (default ON — I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 4).
133	
134	    A chrome non-source (bot/captcha/cookie/404/login/empty — a FAILED FETCH, not a source)
135	    has nothing real to judge for topicality. When ON, such a row is NOT sent to the topic
136	    judge at all: it is KEPT (flows to the downstream content-integrity stamp + chrome-delete
137	    leg) and is NEVER stamped ``topic_off_subject`` / ``topic_offtopic_demoted``, so a garbled
138	    body + a chrome title can never be mislabeled ``confirmed_offtopic`` (Cause 3). OFF =>
139	    byte-identical legacy (every non-exempt row with judgeable text is judged)."""
140	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_JUNK_CHROME_BEFORE_OFFTOPIC", "1").strip().lower()
141	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")

144	# Content fields scanned (widest-first) for the chrome detector — mirrors the
145	# run_honest_sweep_r3 content-integrity stamp pass so the two screens judge the SAME body.
146	_CHROME_BODY_FIELDS = (
147	    "fetched_body", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text", "raw_content",
148	    "raw_text", "page_text", "direct_quote", "statement", "source_text", "body", "text",
149	)

151	
152	def _row_is_chrome_nonsource(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
153	    """True iff the CONTENT-INTEGRITY detector confirms this row is a chrome non-source
154	    (bot/captcha/cookie/404/login/empty). Reads a pre-existing ``content_integrity_junk``
155	    stamp first (cheap), else runs the pure leaf detector on the WIDEST body + url + title
156	    (the long-body Zyte-recovery guard inside the detector protects a real source whose title
157	    is a stale bot page). FAIL-OPEN: any import / error / non-dict => False (row is judged
158	    normally — a detector bug must never silently skip a real source)."""
159	    try:
160	        if not isinstance(row, dict):
161	            return False
162	        v = row.get("content_integrity_junk")
163	        if bool(v) and str(v).strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", ""):
164	            return True
165	        from src.tools.access_bypass import (  # noqa: PLC0415
166	            detect_content_integrity_junk as _detect_ci_junk,
167	        )
168	        body = max(
169	            (str(row.get(_bk) or "") for _bk in _CHROME_BODY_FIELDS),
170	            key=len, default="",
171	        )
172	        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
173	        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or row.get("statement") or "")
174	        is_junk, _cls = _detect_ci_junk(body, url, title)
175	        return bool(is_junk)
176	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a detector defect must never skip a real source
177	        return False
178	
179	
180	def topic_batch_size() -> int:
181	    """``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_BATCH`` (default 25), the max sources per LLM call.
182	    A non-positive / unparseable value falls back to the default (FAIL-SAFE:
183	    a garbage batch size must never produce a zero-size loop)."""
184	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_BATCH", "").strip()
185	    if not raw:
186	        return _DEFAULT_TOPIC_BATCH
187	    try:
188	        value = int(raw)
189	    except ValueError:
190	        return _DEFAULT_TOPIC_BATCH
191	    return value if value > 0 else _DEFAULT_TOPIC_BATCH

208	    # ``kept_rows`` (the source flows to composition) but are disclosed here as

215	def _row_title_text(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
216	    """Title-like text accessor mirroring evidence_selector._row_title_text.
217	
218	    Live evidence rows populate ``statement`` with ``cand.title[:300]`` (not
219	    ``title``). Precedence: explicit ``title`` > ``statement`` >
220	    ``source_title`` > "". Returns a plain string (never None)."""
221	    for key in ("title", "statement", "source_title"):

224	            return v

229	    """Short snippet for the topic judgement. Uses ``snippet`` /
230	    ``direct_quote`` (whichever is present), bounded to _MAX_SNIPPET_CHARS."""
231	    for key in ("snippet", "direct_quote", "summary"):

238	def _row_is_marquee_anchor(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
239	    """True iff the row is a marquee / required-entity anchor that must NOT be
240	    dropped. Mirrors evidence_selector._row_is_marquee_anchor (I-pipe-006
241	    #1231) — a truthy ``is_marquee`` / ``required_entity`` / ``anchor_seed`` /
242	    ``is_anchor`` / ``entity_anchor`` / ``marquee`` flag, OR a
243	    ``required_entity``/``anchor`` substring in ``seed_source`` /
244	    ``query_origin`` / ``seed_query_origin``."""
245	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
246	        return False
247	    for flag in ("is_marquee", "required_entity", "anchor_seed", "is_anchor",
248	                 "entity_anchor", "marquee"):
249	        if row.get(flag):
250	            return True
251	    seed_source = str(row.get("seed_source") or "").lower()
252	    if "required_entity" in seed_source or "anchor" in seed_source:
253	        return True
254	    for origin_key in ("query_origin", "seed_query_origin"):
255	        origin = str(row.get(origin_key) or "").lower()
256	        if "required_entity" in origin or "anchor" in origin:
257	            return True
258	    return False
