1441	def _classify_source_tier_rules(
1442	    signals: ClassificationSignals,
1443	) -> ClassificationResult:
1444	    """Classify a source into T1-T7 (or UNKNOWN) using rules.
1445	
1446	    Rules fire in priority order. The first match wins; later rules
1447	    cannot overturn an earlier decision (so rule ordering is
1448	    load-bearing). Every match appends to result.reasons so the user
1449	    can audit why a tier was assigned.
1450	
1451	    Returns TierLevel.UNKNOWN rather than silent BRONZE when no rule
1452	    matches — see module docstring for rationale.
1453	    """
1454	    result = ClassificationResult(
1455	        tier=TierLevel.UNKNOWN,
1456	        confidence=0.0,
1457	    )
1458	    domain = _normalize_domain(signals.url)
1459	    result.signals_used = {
1460	        "domain": domain,
1461	        "source_type_hint": signals.source_type_hint,
1462	        "publication_type": signals.openalex_publication_type,
1463	        "content_length": signals.fetched_content_length,
1464	    }
1465	
1466	    # ── Rule 0 (BLOCKED): Retracted / withdrawn papers are never classified
1467	    # positively. Fires on the OpenAlex retraction flag OR an explicit
1468	    # retraction / withdrawal / expression-of-concern marker at the START of the
1469	    # title (I-deepfix-001 U10). The title leg catches a retracted paper whose
1470	    # OpenAlex flag is unset because it was re-deposited on a preprint host (it
1471	    # would otherwise earn R7 T4). Excluded (UNKNOWN) — the same treatment the
1472	    # OpenAlex flag already gets; strictly below (worse than) any T-tier.
1473	    _retraction_title = _detect_retraction_marker(signals.title)
1474	    if signals.openalex_is_retracted or _retraction_title:
1475	        result.tier = TierLevel.UNKNOWN  # caller should exclude; not T1-T7
1476	        result.confidence = 1.0
1477	        result.matched_rules.append("R0_retracted")
1478	        _retraction_src = (
1479	            "OpenAlex retraction flag"
1480	            if signals.openalex_is_retracted
1481	            else "explicit retraction / withdrawal marker in the title"
1482	        )
1483	        result.reasons.append(
1484	            f"Paper flagged retracted / withdrawn ({_retraction_src}); excluded "
1485	            f"from tier scoring. Caller should filter retracted sources before "
1486	            f"composition."
1487	        )
1488	        return result
1489	
1490	    # ── Rule Pre-1 (T6, M-18b from DR audit pass 1): Social platform
1491	    # / general-interest portal exclusion. Must fire BEFORE R1 stub-
1492	    # content so that Facebook/Twitter/Reddit pages are classified by
1493	    # domain authority, not by how much text happened to be fetched.
1494	    # A Facebook "Black Box Warning" post is not more citable just
1495	    # because its body happens to be > 1000 chars.
1496	    if _domain_matches(domain, SOCIAL_PLATFORM_DOMAINS):
1497	        result.tier = TierLevel.T6
1498	        result.confidence = 0.98
1499	        result.matched_rules.append("RP1_social_platform_early")
1500	        result.reasons.append(
1501	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a social platform / general-interest "
1502	            f"portal. User-generated or aggregator content; never T1 "
1503	            f"primary research regardless of content length or OpenAlex "
1504	            f"metadata. M-18b: fires BEFORE R1 stub so tier cannot be "
1505	            f"laundered via large body text."
1506	        )
1507	        return result
1508	
1509	    # ── Rule 1 (T7 stub): Tiny content = stub.
1510	    # This runs early because even a JAMA paper fetched as 500-char
1511	    # abstract is effectively a stub for evaluator purposes.
1512	    #
1513	    # I-arch-011 B17/B11 (KEYSTONE) — SEPARATE venue authority from fetch
1514	    # completeness. The original rule demoted to T7 "regardless of venue",
1515	    # which is a regression of I-bug-775 (#815): it dropped real scholarly
1516	    # journals (Lancet / Brain / Movement Disorders) to T7 purely because
1517	    # the fetch returned a short stub, clobbering their domain-aware venue
1518	    # authority. The fix: when the source is a KNOWN SCHOLARLY VENUE (and not
1519	    # a genuine conference abstract, which stays T7 on its own merits), do
1520	    # NOT apply the length floor — let the source flow to the journal/venue
1521	    # rules below and earn its real venue tier — but LABEL it
1522	    # fetch_degraded=True so (a) a re-fetch lane can re-pull the full text and
1523	    # (b) the adequacy lane EXCLUDES it from grounded-content counts. This is
1524	    # WEIGHT-AND-LABEL, never a drop and never a cap: the empty stub keeps its
1525	    # venue authority weight but is barred from counting as grounded content,
1526	    # so venue authority cannot launder a contentless stub into "adequate".
1527	    if signals.fetched_content_length and signals.fetched_content_length < T7_STUB_CONTENT_CHARS:
1528	        if (
1529	            _is_known_scholarly_venue(signals)
1530	            and not _detect_conference_abstract(signals.title, signals.url)
1531	        ):
1532	            # Known journal fetched as a stub: keep venue authority (fall
1533	            # through to the venue rules), but mark the row degraded so the
1534	            # downstream adequacy lane does not count it as grounded content.
1535	            result.fetch_degraded = True
1536	            result.reasons.append(
1537	                f"Fetched body is {signals.fetched_content_length} chars "
1538	                f"(< {T7_STUB_CONTENT_CHARS} threshold) but the source is a "
1539	                f"known scholarly venue: KEEPING venue-authority tier rather "
1540	                f"than demoting to the T7 length floor (I-arch-011 B17). "
1541	                f"Labelled fetch_degraded=True — a re-fetch lane should pull "
1542	                f"the full text and the adequacy lane EXCLUDES this row from "
1543	                f"grounded-content counts until then."
1544	            )
1545	        else:
1546	            result.tier = TierLevel.T7
1547	            result.confidence = 1.0
1548	            result.matched_rules.append("R1_stub_content_length")
1549	            result.reasons.append(
1550	                f"Fetched body is {signals.fetched_content_length} chars "
1551	                f"(< {T7_STUB_CONTENT_CHARS} threshold) — classified T7 stub. "
1552	                f"Not a recognized scholarly venue (or is a conference "
1553	                f"abstract); full-text retrieval would be needed to upgrade."
1554	            )
1555	            return result
1556	
1557	    # ── Rule 2a (T6): Low-provenance document hosts. A government PDF
1558	    # re-hosted on Scribd has unknown authenticity; do NOT elevate to T3
1559	    # just because source_type_hint says "government_report". This fires
1560	    # before the regulatory rule specifically to catch laundering.
1561	    if _domain_matches(domain, LOW_PROVENANCE_HOSTS):
1562	        result.tier = TierLevel.T6
1563	        result.confidence = 0.95
1564	        result.matched_rules.append("R2a_low_provenance_host")
1565	        result.reasons.append(
1566	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a user-upload document host with no "
1567	            f"provenance guarantees. T6 regardless of upstream metadata "
1568	            f"hints; the authentic source should be located at the "
1569	            f"original issuer's URL."
1570	        )
1571	        return result
1572	
1573	    # ── Rule 2b (T6): Legal / consulting commentary
1574	    if _domain_matches(domain, LEGAL_COMMENTARY_DOMAINS):
1575	        result.tier = TierLevel.T6
1576	        result.confidence = 0.95
1577	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_legal_commentary")
1578	        result.reasons.append(
1579	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a law-firm or legal-commentary site. "
1580	            f"Not peer-reviewed research. T6."
1581	        )
1582	        return result
1583	
1584	    # ── Rule 2b-social (T6, BUG-M-7): Social platforms + general-interest
1585	    # portals. Per Codex pass 9 findings, Facebook / Reddit / AOL pages
1586	    # were being classified as T1 via OpenAlex because those domains
1587	    # sometimes appear as journal source_type in upstream metadata.
1588	    # User-generated content and aggregator portals are never T1.
1589	    if _domain_matches(domain, SOCIAL_PLATFORM_DOMAINS):
1590	        result.tier = TierLevel.T6
1591	        result.confidence = 0.95
1592	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_social_platform")
1593	        result.reasons.append(
1594	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a social platform or general-interest "
1595	            f"portal. User-generated or aggregator content; never T1 "
1596	            f"primary research regardless of OpenAlex metadata."
1597	        )
1598	        return result
1599

1600	    # ── Rule 2b-market (T5/T6, BUG-M-7): Market research / consulting.
1601	    # Per Codex pass 9, DelveInsight / Statista / MatrixBCG /
1602	    # PortersFiveForce / PharmaVoice were being classified T1 via
1603	    # OpenAlex. These are paid industry analyses — typically T5 when
1604	    # they're primary market research, T6 when they're strategy-blog
1605	    # summaries. Tier at T5 by default (industry-funded) because most
1606	    # specific reports cited in POLARIS runs are paid research, not
1607	    # vendor marketing collateral.
1608	    if _domain_matches(domain, MARKET_RESEARCH_DOMAINS):
1609	        result.tier = TierLevel.T5
1610	        result.confidence = 0.9
1611	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_market_research")
1612	        result.reasons.append(
1613	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a market-research / consulting firm. "
1614	            f"Paid industry analysis, not peer-reviewed research. T5."
1615	        )
1616	        return result
1617	
1618	    # ── Rule 2b-clinref (T4, BUG-M-10): Clinical reference products.
1619	    # UpToDate, DynaMed, ClinicalKey, BMJ Best Practice, etc. are
1620	    # clinical decision-support summaries. Useful for practitioners
1621	    # but not primary research. Tier at T4 (narrative / commentary).
1622	    if _domain_matches(domain, CLINICAL_REFERENCE_PRODUCTS):
1623	        result.tier = TierLevel.T4
1624	        result.confidence = 0.9
1625	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_clinical_reference_product")
1626	        result.reasons.append(
1627	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a clinical reference product (UpToDate "
1628	            f"/ DynaMed / etc.). Practitioner-oriented decision-support "
1629	            f"summaries of existing evidence, not primary research. T4."
1630	        )
1631	        return result
1632	
1633	    # ── Rule 2b-policy (T4, BUG-M-10): Policy think-tanks / advocacy.
1634	    # KFF, Commonwealth Fund, Brookings, Rand, PhRMA, etc. produce
1635	    # policy analyses, explainers, and advocacy content. Useful
1636	    # references but not peer-reviewed primary research. Tier at T4
1637	    # as narrative analysis by default.
1638	    if _domain_matches(domain, POLICY_THINK_TANK_DOMAINS):
1639	        result.tier = TierLevel.T4
1640	        result.confidence = 0.85
1641	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_policy_think_tank")
1642	        result.reasons.append(
1643	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a policy think-tank / advocacy / "
1644	            f"trade-association site. Policy analysis or explainer "
1645	            f"content, not peer-reviewed primary research. T4."
1646	        )
1647	        return result
1648	
1649	    # ── Rule 2b-gov-agency (T3, BUG-M-10): non-regulatory government
1650	    # agency content. CMS.gov, HHS.gov etc. produce administrative,
1651	    # policy, and fact-sheet content. T3 government/regulatory is
1652	    # correct here — but crucially NOT T1 primary research via
1653	    # OpenAlex metadata misclassification.
1654	    if _domain_matches(domain, GOV_AGENCY_DOMAINS):
1655	        result.tier = TierLevel.T3
1656	        result.confidence = 0.95
1657	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_gov_agency")
1658	        result.reasons.append(
1659	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a US government agency (non-regulatory). "
1660	            f"Administrative / policy / fact-sheet content, not primary "
1661	            f"research. T3."
1662	        )
1663	        return result
1664	
1665	    # ── Rule 2b-stat-agency (T3, I-ready-017 #1133): national +
1666	    # international statistical / data agencies (BLS, OECD, ILO, Eurostat,
1667	    # StatCan, World Bank, IMF, Federal Reserve, FRED, Census). These
1668	    # produce PRIMARY quantitative evidence and are the expected T3
1669	    # backbone for non-clinical domains (workforce protocol requires
1670	    # T3 at 35-65%). Placed adjacent to R2b_gov_agency / R2c / R2d so
1671	    # statistical agencies earn T3 the same way regulatory domains do,
1672	    # and crucially BEFORE R9/R10/R11 (the OpenAlex paths that demoted
1673	    # bls.gov to T4) and AFTER the R2a/R2b denylist demotions (so a
1674	    # denylisted domain can never be laundered up to T3). It is T3
1675	    # (government/regulatory/authoritative-data tier), NOT T1 primary-
1676	    # research-paper credit.
1677	    if _domain_matches(domain, STATISTICAL_AGENCY_DOMAINS):
1678	        result.tier = TierLevel.T3
1679	        result.confidence = 0.95
1680	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_statistical_agency")
1681	        result.reasons.append(
1682	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a national or international statistical / "
1683	            f"data agency (e.g. BLS, OECD, ILO, Eurostat, StatCan, World "
1684	            f"Bank, IMF, Federal Reserve, Census). Authoritative primary "
1685	            f"quantitative evidence — T3 regardless of OpenAlex metadata "
1686	            f"(which mis-labelled BLS reports as preprint/repository or "
1687	            f"narrative review). Not T1 primary-research-paper credit."
1688	        )
1689	        return result
1690	
1691	    # ── Rule 2b-bizness (T6, BUG-M-10): Business / general news.
1692	    # Fast Company, Forbes, etc. When OpenAlex mis-labels them as
1693	    # 'article'/'journal', they should still be T6 news.
1694	    if _domain_matches(domain, BUSINESS_NEWS_DOMAINS):
1695	        result.tier = TierLevel.T6
1696	        result.confidence = 0.95
1697	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_business_news")
1698	        result.reasons.append(
1699	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a business / general news publisher. "
1700	            f"Not peer-reviewed research. T6."
1701	        )
1702	        return result
1703	
1704	    # ── Rule 2b-webguide (T6, BUG-M-10): SEO / web-guide content.
1705	    # Chitika, PCMag, etc. Consumer-style "best X of year" articles.
1706	    if _domain_matches(domain, WEB_GUIDE_DOMAINS):
1707	        result.tier = TierLevel.T6
1708	        result.confidence = 0.95
1709	        result.matched_rules.append("R2b_web_guide")
1710	        result.reasons.append(
1711	            f"Domain {domain!r} is an SEO / consumer web-guide site. T6."
1712	        )
1713	        return result
1714	
1715	    # ── Rule 2c (T3): Industry-hosted regulatory content (product
1716	    # monographs, prescribing information) — overrides industry-marketing
1717	    # classification because the CONTENT is a regulatory-approved label
1718	    # even though the HOSTING is manufacturer-controlled.
1719	    _url_lower = (signals.url or "").lower()
1720	    _title_lower = (signals.title or "").lower()
1721	    _regulatory_content_markers = (
1722	        "product-monograph", "product_monograph",
1723	        "prescribing-information", "prescribing_information",
1724	        "215256s",  # FDA label revision pattern
1725	    )
1726	    _regulatory_title_markers = (
1727	        "product monograph", "prescribing information",
1728	    )
1729	    if any(m in _url_lower for m in _regulatory_content_markers) or \
1730	       any(m in _title_lower for m in _regulatory_title_markers):
1731	        result.tier = TierLevel.T3
1732	        result.confidence = 0.9
1733	        result.matched_rules.append("R2c_regulatory_content_marker")
1734	        result.reasons.append(
1735	            "URL or title contains regulatory-content marker (product "
1736	            "monograph / prescribing information / FDA label revision "
1737	            "pattern). T3 regardless of hosting domain."
1738	        )
1739	        return result
1740	
1741	    # ── Rule 2d (T3): Regulatory domains
1742	    if _domain_matches(domain, REGULATORY_DOMAINS):
1743	        result.tier = TierLevel.T3
1744	        result.confidence = 1.0
1745	        result.matched_rules.append("R2d_regulatory_domain")
1746	        result.reasons.append(
1747	            f"Domain {domain!r} matches regulatory body list. "
1748	            f"T3 regulatory/government source."
1749	        )
1750	        return result
1751	
1752	    # ── Rule 3 (T5): Pharmaceutical-industry HCP portals / brand sites
1753	    # (This fires BEFORE peer-reviewed-journal rule because some pharma
1754	    # companies host their own pseudo-journal content.)
1755	    if _domain_matches(domain, INDUSTRY_MARKETING_DOMAINS):
1756	        result.tier = TierLevel.T5
1757	        result.confidence = 1.0
1758	        result.matched_rules.append("R3_industry_marketing_domain")
1759	        result.reasons.append(
1760	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a pharmaceutical-industry HCP portal or "
1761	            f"brand site. Industry marketing material — T5 regardless of "
1762	            f"OpenAlex indexing. (Peer-reviewed papers from the same "
1763	            f"company published in a journal should be cited via the "
1764	            f"journal URL, where they classify to T1/T2/T4.)"
1765	        )
1766	        return result
1767	
1768	    # ── Rule 3b (T5): Branded physician-portal commentary (touchX, medscape)
1769	    if _domain_matches(domain, PHYSICIAN_PORTAL_COMMENTARY_DOMAINS):
1770	        result.tier = TierLevel.T5
1771	        result.confidence = 0.9
1772	        result.matched_rules.append("R3b_physician_portal_commentary")
1773	        result.reasons.append(
1774	            f"Domain {domain!r} is a branded physician-portal commentary "

2260	            result.reasons.append(

2306	    if pub_type in ("book-chapter", "book", "dataset", "editorial", "letter"):

2316	    result.tier = TierLevel.UNKNOWN

2329	def classify_url(url: str, content_length: int = 0, **extra: Any) -> ClassificationResult:
2330	    """Classify a source by URL + content length + optional extras."""

2339	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

2351	
2352	
2353	def _classify_via_authority_model(

2356	    """ON path: compute authority + render the clinical T1-T7 VIEW.

2358	    Returns a ClassificationResult with the five legacy fields populated off the
2359	    rendered view PLUS the four additive authority fields and

2372	    authority_result = score_source_authority(signals)

2374	        ClinicalViewInput(

2377	            is_retracted=signals.openalex_is_retracted,
2378	            fetched_content_length=signals.fetched_content_length,
2379	            title=signals.title,

2384	    # ── I-arch-011 B17/B11 (KEYSTONE) — un-stub a known scholarly venue on the
2385	    # authority-model path too. The renderer's `stub_content_t7` rule demotes

2412	                fetched_content_length=T7_STUB_CONTENT_CHARS,  # lift above stub
2413	                title=signals.title,

2416	        )
2417	        if unstubbed_tier_str != "T7":
2418	            # The T7 was a length artifact, not a real abstract: restore the
